equations.name: Total HTC Hamiltonian

variables.meaning: cavity photon Hamiltonian, = hbar*omega_c * a^dag a (zero-point energy neglected)
molecular subsystem Hamiltonian of the jth donor-acceptor pair

equations.context: Ensemble of N non-interacting molecules coupled to a single quantized cavity mode; long-wavelength approximation.
equations.origin: Unraveling abnormal collective effects via the non-monotonic number dependence of electron transfer in confined electromagnetic fields

equations.name: Molecular (spin-boson) Hamiltonian per pair
equations.latex: \hat{H}_{M,j} = E_D |D_j\rangle\langle D_j| + \left(E_A + \sum_\alpha c_{\alpha,j}(\hat{b}^\dagger_{j,\alpha} + \hat{b}_{j,\alpha})\right)|A_j\rangle\langle A_j| + H_{AD}|A_j\rangle\langle D_j| + H_{DA}|D_j\rangle\langle A_j| + \sum_\alpha \hbar\omega_\alpha\left(\hat{b}^\dagger_{j,\alpha}\hat{b}_{j,\alpha} + \tfrac{1}{2}\right)

variables.meaning: diabatic donor / acceptor electronic states of jth pair (excess electron on donor / acceptor)
electronic (diabatic) coupling between donor and acceptor; H_{AD} = <A_j|H_{M,j}|D_j>
linear coupling of acceptor electronic state to boson mode alpha

equations.context: Spin-boson model for each D-A pair; nuclear modes treated semiclassically as classical harmonic oscillators (primary mode + thermal bath). Identical pairs.
equations.origin: Unraveling abnormal collective effects via the non-monotonic number dependence of electron transfer in confined electromagnetic fields

equations.name: Molecular Hamiltonian matrix (semiclassical, primary coordinate)

variables.meaning: classical position and momentum of the primary nuclear mode of pair j (effective mass m absorbed)
donor / acceptor diabatic potential energy surfaces (see Eq. 4)

equations.context: Primary mode R_j follows the Langevin equation; obtained after recasting bath into primary mode + secondary thermal bath.
equations.origin: Unraveling abnormal collective effects via the non-monotonic number dependence of electron transfer in confined electromagnetic fields

equations.name: Diabatic potential energy surfaces
equations.latex: V_D(R_j) = E_D + \tfrac{1}{2}\omega_v^2 R_j^2, \qquad V_A(R_j) = E_A + \lambda_v R_j + \tfrac{1}{2}\omega_v^2 R_j^2

variables.meaning: characteristic frequency of the primary vibrational mode (j-independent)
variables.typical: 80.6 cm^-1 (baseline); varied over slow/fast bath in Fig.4

variables.meaning: primary-mode (system-bath) coupling strength; sets reorganization energy
variables.typical: lambda_v^2 = 2*omega_v^2 in Fig.4; E_r = 1.0 eV baseline

variables.meaning: donor-acceptor energy gap E_AD = E_A - E_D

equations.context: Harmonic diabatic PES with identical curvature; reorganization energy E_r = lambda_v^2/(2 omega_v^2).

equations.name: Langevin equation for primary mode

variables.typical: gamma = 0.065 s^-1 (as printed in paper; see uncertainty)

variables.meaning: Markovian Gaussian stochastic random force (white noise)

equations.where: p.3 (unnumbered Langevin eq.) and surrounding text
equations.context: Note: paper writes the friction term as -gamma_v * P-dot (transcribed as printed); standard Langevin would have -gamma_v * R-dot. Records the local thermal fluctuation of jth molecule.

equations.name: Cavity field at molecule

equations.name: Electric dipole coupling Hamiltonian
equations.latex: \hat{H}_{DE} = \hbar\omega_c(\hat{a} - \hat{a}^\dagger)\sum_j\left[ g_D|D_j\rangle\langle D_j| + g_A|A_j\rangle\langle A_j| + t_{AD}|A_j\rangle\langle D_j| + t_{DA}|D_j\rangle\langle A_j| \right]

variables.meaning: dimensionless dipole couplings for donor / acceptor diagonal dipoles
dimensionless radiative coupling for the D-A transition dipole

equations.context: Idealized: N molecules equally coupled to cavity, no spatial/orientational disorder, no intermolecular dipole-dipole.

equations.name: Dimensionless coupling definitions
equations.context: Dipole moments j-independent (identical molecules).
equations.origin: Unraveling abnormal collective effects via the non-monotonic number dependence of electron transfer in confined electromagnetic fields

equations.name: Dipole self-energy term
equations.latex: \hat{H}_{DS} = \hbar\omega_c\sum_j\left[|g_D|^2|D_j\rangle\langle D_j| + |g_A|^2|A_j\rangle\langle A_j| + |t_{AD}|^2\right] - \hbar\omega_c(g_D + g_A)\sum_j\left[t_{AD}|A_j\rangle\langle D_j| + t_{DA}|D_j\rangle\langle A_j|\right]
equations.context: Self-energy does NOT involve photon absorption/emission (local molecular term); second term gives an effective contribution to the diabatic coupling, affecting ET. Important in strong-coupling regime.
equations.origin: Unraveling abnormal collective effects via the non-monotonic number dependence of electron transfer in confined electromagnetic fields

equations.name: Polaron transformation generator
equations.latex: \hat{\mathcal{S}} = \sum_j\left( g_D^*\hat{a}^\dagger - g_D\hat{a}\right)|D_j\rangle\langle D_j| + \left( g_A^*\hat{a}^\dagger - g_A\hat{a}\right)|A_j\rangle\langle A_j|, \qquad \hat{H} = e^{\hat{\mathcal{S}}}\hat{H}e^{-\hat{\mathcal{S}}}

variables.meaning: anti-Hermitian generator of polaron (Lang-Firsov-type) unitary transformation

equations.context: Eliminates the quadratic light-matter coupling; cavity Hamiltonian H_C unchanged.

equations.name: Photonic shift (Franck-Condon) operator

equations.name: Dressed-state diagonal PES

variables.meaning: photon number (restricted to m=0,1 in this work)

equations.name: Marcus ET rate (cavity-modified, general)

equations.name: ET rates from polariton states RWA-(I) absorption

equations.name: ET rates from polariton states RWA-(II) emission

equations.name: Polariton relaxation (PR) rate, Fermi golden rule
equations.context: Note: paper writes sum k=1..N-1 but dark states are k=1..N-2 (see uncertainty). PR is relaxation of polariton to dark states via local molecular fluctuations, not cavity loss.

equations.name: Brownian spectral density (PR)

equations.name: PR rates absorption RWA-(I)

equations.name: PR rates emission RWA-(II)
equations.context: Emission-case PR. At resonance Gamma_+^(I)=Gamma_-^(I) and Gamma_+^(II)=Gamma_-^(II); PR ~ N^{-2} for large N.

equations.name: Resonance Rabi splitting (Delta=0)
equations.context: At resonance Delta=0, cos theta = sin theta = 1/sqrt(2), independent of N. Rabi splitting increases as sqrt(N-1).

equations.name: N_max turnover (barrier-less condition) — CENTRAL RESULT

variables.meaning: molecule number at which the photon-coupled ET path becomes barrier-less (maximum ET rate); turnover point
variables.typical: for hbar*omega_c=1.0 eV, |T_01|=69 cm^-1 ~ 8.55e-3 eV => N_max ~ 1 + 117 ~ 118 (order 1e2-1e3 as in Fig.2)

variables.meaning: magnitude of effective photon-coupled diabatic coupling (= hbar*omega_c*|t_AD| when g_AD=0)

equations.context: Derived by setting Omega_+^(1)-hbar*omega_c=0 (abs.) or Omega_-^(II)+hbar*omega_c=0 (emis.) at resonance so the ET path activation energy vanishes (barrier-less). IMPORTANT: the printed N_max formula depends ONLY on hbar*omega_c and |T_01|/|T_10|; E_AD enters implicitly through the resonance condition |E_AD|=hbar*omega_c used in deriving Omega_pm and is NOT an explicit variable in Eq.(44). For large N>>N_max both ET path (b),(c) decrease with N, asymptotic ET rate k^(I) propto k^(II) propto e^{-N}; PR rates scale as N^{-2}.
equations.origin: Unraveling abnormal collective effects via the non-monotonic number dependence of electron transfer in confined electromagnetic fields

equations.name: Cavity-induced quantum yields RWA-(I)

variables.meaning: quantum yields of path (b) and path (c) (ratio of final acceptor population, from polaritons, to cavity photon occupation)

equations.context: QY excludes standard ET path (a); counts only cavity-modified acceptor population. QY_b ~ N (linear, Dicke) for small N; suppressed for large N. QY_c < 0.5 because only one polariton contributes.
equations.origin: Unraveling abnormal collective effects via the non-monotonic number dependence of electron transfer in confined electromagnetic fields

equations.name: Driven-dissipative Gross-Pitaevskii / complex Ginzburg-Landau equation (cGLE) for the condensate

variables.typical: ~10^-4 to 10^-5 electron masses (GaAs microcavity; not numerically given in paper)
not given numerically (enters via dimensionless g=2 g_R/R_R)

variables.meaning: energy relaxation coefficient (rate gain decreases with increasing energy)
rate reservoir excitons enter (stimulate into) the condensate
variables.units: um^2 / s (hbar R_R given as meV um^2)

variables.meaning: rate of condensate losses (photon decay through Bragg mirrors)

equations.context: Mean-field driven-dissipative model of a non-resonantly pumped polariton condensate coupled to a hot exciton reservoir. Valid in the strong-coupling polariton regime under incoherent (non-resonant) pumping.
equations.origin: A polariton graph simulator

equations.name: Reservoir rate equation

variables.meaning: redistribution rate of reservoir excitons between energy levels (reservoir decay)

variables.typical: spatial profile set by pump laser

variables.meaning: reservoir density (stationary R = P/(gamma_R + R_R|psi|^2) in limit gamma_R >> gamma_C)

equations.context: Coupled to eq (1). The sign convention as printed; in the limit gamma_R >> gamma_C the stationary reservoir R = P(r)/(gamma_R + R_R|psi|^2) is used.
equations.origin: A polariton graph simulator

equations.name: Dimensionless cGLE with saturable nonlinearity
equations.latex: i\frac{\partial \Psi}{\partial t} = -(1 - i\eta n_R)\nabla^2 \Psi + |\Psi|^2 \Psi + g n_R \Psi + i(n_R - \gamma)\Psi

variables.meaning: rescaled condensate wavefunction (psi -> sqrt(hbar^2/(2 m U_0 l0^2)) Psi)
dimensionless condensate loss gamma = m gamma_C l0^2/hbar
dimensionless energy relaxation eta = eta_d hbar/(m R_R l0^2)
dimensionless pump p = m l0^2 R_R P(r)/(hbar gamma_R)
dimensionless reservoir saturation b = R_R hbar^2/(2 m l0^2 gamma_R U_0)

equations.context: Non-dimensionalized form of eqs (1)-(2) with saturable (reservoir-clamped) nonlinearity n_R = p/(1+b|Psi|^2). Length scale l0 = 1 um, time -> 2 m t l0^2/hbar.
equations.origin: A polariton graph simulator

equations.name: Standard cGLE (Taylor-expanded reservoir, small |Psi|^2)
equations.latex: i\frac{\partial \Psi}{\partial t} = -(1 - i\eta p)\nabla^2 \Psi + (1 - g p b)|\Psi|^2 \Psi + g p \Psi + i(p - \gamma - p b|\Psi|^2)\Psi

variables.meaning: dimensionless pump profile p(r)=p0 exp(-sigma r^2)
dimensionless interaction, saturation, loss, relaxation parameters

equations.context: Obtained by Taylor expansion of n_R for small |Psi|^2; the g eta|Psi|^2 term dropped given smallness of eta. This is the equation actually solved numerically and analytically (Fig 2).
equations.origin: A polariton graph simulator

equations.name: Mass continuity equation (Madelung form, Psi = sqrt(rho) exp(iS), u = grad S)
equations.context: Real part of the radially symmetric steady-state cGLE (3)-(4). Madelung transformation Psi = sqrt(rho) exp(iS).
equations.origin: A polariton graph simulator

equations.name: Bernoulli equation (chemical potential)

variables.typical: mu = k_c^2 - gamma^2/(4 k_c^2) at infinity

equations.context: Imaginary part / phase equation of the radially symmetric steady-state cGLE. Away from pump (p=0): mu = k_c^2 - gamma^2/(4 k_c^2).
equations.origin: A polariton graph simulator

equations.name: Approximate condensate density profile
equations.latex: \rho(r) = \frac{a_0}{\gamma r \exp(\gamma r k_c^{-1}) k_c^{-1} + \xi - \gamma r/k_c + a_3 r^3}

variables.meaning: parametric-family fitting parameters determined by matched asymptotics (eqs 10-13)

equations.context: Approximate analytic density built from origin (r->0) and infinity (rho ~ exp(-r gamma/k_c)/r) asymptotics. Used for single-condensate wavefunction in Fig 2 and Fig 3.
equations.origin: A polariton graph simulator

equations.name: Approximate condensate velocity profile
equations.context: Outflow velocity grows linearly near origin and saturates to k_c at infinity. k_c, l found via matched asymptotics (eqs 10-13).
equations.origin: A polariton graph simulator

equations.name: Parameters xi and a_3 (leading order)
equations.context: Leading-order expansion of eq (6) and first-order of eq (7) about r=0.
equations.origin: A polariton graph simulator

equations.name: Outflow wavenumber k_c^2 (matched-asymptotics expansion of eq 6 to O(r^2))
equations.latex: k_c^2 = \frac{4 a_0 b l^3 p_0 (2l + \gamma) + 3\gamma^2(8 l^3 - 12 l^2(p_0 - \gamma) + (p_0 - \gamma)^2 \gamma) + 2l(2 p_0^2 - 5 p_0 \gamma + 3\gamma^2)}{3 a_0 b p_0 \sigma(2l + \gamma)^2}
equations.context: Determines k_c from system + pump parameters. NOTE: grouping of polynomial terms should be re-verified against PDF (see uncertainties).
equations.origin: A polariton graph simulator

equations.name: Closure relations for k_c^2 and l^2 (O(r^2) expansion of eq 7)
equations.latex: k_c^2 = \frac{a_0}{\xi} + \frac{\gamma^2(\xi + 8)}{4 k_c^2 \xi} + \frac{g p_0 \xi}{a_0 b + \xi}, \qquad l^2 = \frac{a_0 \gamma^2}{k_c^2 \xi^2} + \frac{5\gamma^4}{k_c^4 \xi} - \frac{4\gamma^4}{3 k_c^4 \xi} - \frac{a_0 b g p_0 \gamma^2}{k_c^2(a_0 b + \xi)^2} + \frac{g p_0 \sigma \xi}{a_0 b + \xi}
equations.context: Two coupled nonlinear equations that, with eqs (10)-(11), fully determine a_0, l, xi, a_3, k_c for given g, b, gamma, p0, sigma.
equations.origin: A polariton graph simulator

equations.name: Single-condensate wavefunction (phase from integrated velocity)
equations.context: Phase S(r) = (k_c^2/l) log(cosh(l r/k_c)) obtained by integrating u(r)=k_c tanh(l r/k_c). Building block for the N-condensate ansatz Psi_N approx sum_i Psi_0(|r-r_i|) exp(i theta_i).
equations.origin: A polariton graph simulator

equations.name: Multi-spot Gaussian pump profile

variables.meaning: pump intensity at centre of spot i
variables.typical: p_i = p0 for all i (p0=3 in Fig 3)

position of pump spot / graph vertex i

equations.name: Total mass with XY coupling term (the analogue XY mapping)

variables.meaning: total number of polaritons (total mass); the system maximizes this
coupling between sites i and j (eq 19); sign tunable
phase of condensate i (XY spin angle)

equations.context: Maximizing M is equivalent to minimizing H_XY = -sum_{i<j} J_ij cos(theta_ij). Central result establishing the polariton graph as an XY simulator. Wavefunctions assumed well-separated.

equations.name: Coupling coefficient J_ij (Bessel overlap integral)

variables.meaning: Hankel transform of single-condensate wavefunction, = 2pi integral Psi_0(r) J_0(kr) r dr
zeroth-order Bessel function of the first kind

equations.context: Main contribution from k=k_c. The oscillatory J_0(k_c d) makes J_ij alternate sign with separation d -> tunable ferro/antiferromagnetic coupling. This is the microscopic origin of the XY couplings.

equations.name: Classical XY Hamiltonian (minimized by the polariton graph)
equations.context: The global ground state of the polariton graph (max M) corresponds to the minimum of H_XY. The simulator targets this NP-hard minimization for non-convex/dense J.
equations.origin: A polariton graph simulator

equations.name: Two-mode model (network of radiatively coupled condensates)

variables.meaning: complex amplitude of condensate i (no spatial variation)
radiative coupling between condensates i and j (from interference of outflowing polaritons)

equations.context: Spatially-averaged (two-mode) reduction representing each condensate as a single mode coupled radiatively. Basis for the Kuramoto derivation.

equations.name: Density and phase dynamics of coupled condensates
equations.context: Real/imaginary parts of eq (20) with blueshift g neglected and gamma=1. Density steady state rho=(p-1)/b.
equations.origin: A polariton graph simulator

equations.name: Kuramoto model for condensate phases
equations.context: Fast-density limit (rho_i = rho = (p-1)/b). Gradient flow that minimizes V(theta) and hence the XY Hamiltonian. Natural frequencies degenerate for identical pumping.

equations.name: Kuramoto potential energy landscape

variables.meaning: potential whose minima are the Kuramoto equilibria (XY ground states)

equations.context: theta_dot_i = -dV/dtheta_i; gradient flow to minima of V minimizes XY Hamiltonian.

equations.name: Two-condensate phase oscillation (damped) equation

variables.meaning: relative phase of the two condensates
radiative coupling between the two spots

equations.context: Two-spot reduction (R=(rho1+rho2)/2, z=(rho1-rho2)/2, R=(p-1)/b to leading order). Oscillations damp at rate 2(1 - 1/p + J12 cos theta12); phase locks to 0 for J12>0, pi for J12<0, matching H_XY = -J12 cos theta12.
equations.origin: A polariton graph simulator

equations.name: Single-molecule coupling strength g_c

variables.units: rad/s (or eV when multiplied by hbar)
variables.typical: tens to hundreds of cm^-1; ~meV per molecule in FP cavities

variables.meaning: permittivity inside cavity (= epsilon_0 in vacuum)

equations.context: Single emitter coupled to one cavity mode; derived rigorously from minimal coupling in Section 2.5. Defines coupling in the Jaynes-Cummings model.

equations.name: Jaynes-Cummings Hamiltonian
equations.context: Single two-level molecule + single cavity mode under rotating-wave approximation (RWA), dipole approximation, two-level approximation, no permanent dipole, no DSE. Good qualitative picture only; breaks down for realistic molecules and ultrastrong coupling.

equations.name: JC polariton eigenstates and mixing angle
equations.context: Single-molecule JC model. At resonance (Delta-E = hbar omega_c) the mixing angle is pi/4 giving 50/50 light-matter hybrid states.

equations.name: JC polariton eigenenergies and Rabi splitting

variables.meaning: upper(+)/lower(-) polariton energy for n photons
Rabi splitting = energy gap between UP and LP

equations.context: Single-molecule JC. At resonance the Rabi splitting = hbar Omega_n = 2 hbar g_c sqrt(n+1), scaling with sqrt of photon number.

equations.name: Collective (Tavis-Cummings) Rabi splitting

variables.typical: 0.1-1 eV electronic; up to ~100s cm^-1 VSC

equations.context: N identical two-level molecules collectively coupled to one cavity mode (Tavis-Cummings). The sqrt(N) (or sqrt(concentration)) scaling is the hallmark collective effect, verified experimentally.
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: Collective bright state, polaritons, and dark states

variables.meaning: all-ground matter state with 1 cavity photon

equations.context: Single-excitation manifold of the Tavis-Cummings model for identical, non-interacting, disorder-free molecules. Only the bright state couples to the photon; the N-1 dark states have zero net transition dipole (optically dark) and zero photonic character.
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: Minimal coupling (p.A) Hamiltonian, Coulomb gauge

variables.meaning: canonical momentum of charged particle j
charge and mass of particle j (electrons and nuclei)
transverse vector potential = A_0(a-hat + a-hat^dagger), A_0 = sqrt(hbar/2 omega_c eps_0 V) e-hat

equations.context: Long-wavelength, single-mode approximation, Coulomb gauge. Light-matter coupling carried through matter momentum + vector potential. Requires more matter states to converge than dipole gauge under truncation.
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: Dipole-gauge (d.E) Hamiltonian

variables.meaning: total molecular dipole operator = Sum_j z_j x_j

equations.context: Obtained from p.A via Power-Zienau-Woolley (PZW) transformation. Length/dipole gauge. The (mu.A_0)^2 DSE term arises only from field quantization and is required for a bounded ground state.
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: Pauli-Fierz (PF) Hamiltonian (dipole gauge, q_c/p_c form)
equations.context: Purely real Hamiltonian (long-wavelength approx). Obtained from d.E via U_0 = exp(-i pi/2 a^dagger a). The photonic DOF behaves as an additional 'nuclear coordinate' displaced by sqrt(2/hbar omega_c) mu.A_0. Standard form used for ab initio polariton chemistry.
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: PF Hamiltonian, ab initio / lambda form

variables.typical: set by mode volume; cavity-volume dependent

equations.context: Convention used in ab initio polariton literature. lambda = sqrt(hbar/eps_0 V) is the magnitude of the vector potential times sqrt(2 omega_c); A_0 = lambda/sqrt(2 omega_c).
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: Quantum Rabi Hamiltonian (no DSE, no RWA)
equations.context: Two-level molecule + single mode, ignoring permanent dipole and DSE, keeping counter-rotating terms. JC obtained by dropping counter-rotating terms; Rabi overestimates negative energy corrections, incorrectly destabilizing the ground state without DSE.
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: Tavis-Cummings Hamiltonian (N molecules)
equations.context: Many identical two-level molecules + one cavity mode, RWA, dipole approximation, no permanent dipole, no DSE. Analytical solution in single-excitation subspace gives 2 polaritons + N-1 dark states.
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: Holstein-Tavis-Cummings (HTC) Hamiltonian

variables.meaning: phonon (Holstein) coupling strength for molecule J, mode nu
molecular excitation projector for molecule J

equations.context: Tavis-Cummings extended with phenomenological phonon (Holstein) modes linearly coupled to molecular excitation. Standard model for collective polariton dynamics with vibronic coupling; used heavily in dynamics simulations.
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: Generalized Tavis-Cummings (GTC) Hamiltonian (many modes, many molecules)

variables.typical: k_z = n_z pi / L_z quantized; k_x, k_y quasi-continuous

equations.context: Many molecules coupled to many cavity modes (Fabry-Perot dispersion). Reduces to standard TC when removing phase factors (long-wavelength) and single mode. Used for angle-resolved dispersion simulations.
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: Fabry-Perot cavity photon dispersion

variables.meaning: incident angle of photon from mirror normal (tan theta = k_x/k_z)

equations.context: Planar Fabry-Perot microcavity, single k_z. At theta=0, E_ph = hbar c k_z/n_eff = hbar omega_c. Quadratic dispersion in angle gives the parabolic cavity branch observed in angle-resolved spectra.
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: Angle/k-resolved polariton dispersion and Hopfield coefficients
equations.context: Tavis-Cummings model evaluated mode-by-mode for the FP dispersion; gives the band-bending (anticrossing) of polariton branches with incident angle. Hopfield coefficients quantify light/matter mixing.
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: Dipole self-energy (DSE)
equations.context: Required term in d.E and PF Hamiltonians. Ensures bounded ground state and Maxwell equations in matter. Negligible for small coupling (g_c << omega_c) but essential in (ultra)strong coupling. Absent in classical/semiclassical treatments because it arises from field commutation relations.
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: Unitless coupling parameter (regimes)
equations.context: Defines coupling regime. JC accurate for eta < 0.1; collective version g_c sqrt(N)/omega_c sets the ultrastrong/deep-strong boundary.
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: VSC model Hamiltonian (single molecule, reaction coordinate + cavity)

variables.meaning: ground-state PES along R (e.g. double well)
ground-state permanent dipole as function of R

equations.context: Single molecule in vibrational strong coupling: ground electronic state, reaction coordinate R coupled to one cavity mode q_c through the permanent dipole, plus a dissipative bath. Cavity acts as a solvent-like DOF. Resonant when omega_c = omega_0 (reactant well frequency).
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: VSC single-molecule Rabi splitting

variables.meaning: reduced mass of reaction coordinate R
slope of permanent dipole at reactant well

equations.context: Single-molecule VSC; coupling enters through the dipole derivative (IR-active vibration). Valid under linear dipole approximation; generalizes to collective form eq 220.
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: Collective VSC Rabi splitting (N molecules)

variables.meaning: number of molecules coupled to q_c
collective (bright) molecular coordinate that couples to q_c

equations.context: N identical molecules collectively coupled to one cavity mode in VSC; the collective bright coordinate couples with sqrt(N). Only valid under linear dipole approximation; breaks for ultrastrong (eta > 0.1).
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: Polariton-mediated charge transfer: effective electronic coupling (1/sqrt(N))

variables.meaning: state with molecule J in donor excited state, 0 photons

equations.context: Collective polariton-mediated electron transfer. The 1/sqrt(2N) prefactor causes the polariton reorganization energy to scale as 1/N (polaron decoupling) and the effective coupling to scale as 1/sqrt(N) per acceptor.
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: Marcus rate for polariton-mediated charge transfer
equations.context: Marcus (Fermi golden rule) rate from polariton |+/-> to acceptor states. Cavity tunes both driving force and reorganization energy; the 1/N reorganization scaling is the key collective effect.
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: Transition state theory rate for VSC (cavity-independent barrier)
equations.context: Simple 1D TST for VSC. Because the PF light-matter term is a complete square in q_c, the barrier is independent of cavity frequency and coupling, so TST predicts NO VSC rate modification, demonstrating TST cannot explain VSC experiments.
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: Rate with dynamical transmission coefficient (Grote-Hynes / PGH)

variables.meaning: stable normal-mode frequencies at reactant well

equations.context: The cavity modifies the ground-state rate through the transmission coefficient (dynamical caging / recrossing), NOT the barrier. GH captures the rate suppression; PGH (eq 204) extends to both energy-diffusion (enhancement) and spatial-diffusion (suppression) regimes.
equations.origin: Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics

equations.name: Collective vacuum Rabi splitting (single-mode, N identical two-level molecules)

variables.meaning: vacuum Rabi splitting (energy gap between upper and lower polariton on resonance)
number of identical molecules coupled to the single EM mode
molecular transition dipole moment (lowest two electronic states)
vacuum (single-photon) electric field amplitude of the confined mode
photon energy of the cavity mode (≈ molecular transition energy on resonance)
relative background permittivity of the molecular material
effective mode volume of the confined EM field
variables.typical: below 100 nm^3, down to ~1 nm^3 for picocavities; wavelength^3 scale for optical

equations.context: Simplified model: N identical two-level molecules, all coupled identically to a single EM mode, space-dependent field profile ignored, rovibrational motion ignored. Leads to Omega_R proportional to sqrt(mu^2 N / V_eff), i.e. proportional to sqrt(dipole density) — independent of absolute molecule number or cavity geometry at fixed density.
equations.origin: Theoretical Challenges in Polaritonic Chemistry

equations.name: Full non-relativistic QED coupled Hamiltonian (Coulomb gauge)

variables.meaning: kinetic energies and Coulomb interactions of the charged particles (electrons and nuclei); the standard quantum-chemistry Hamiltonian
radiative (transversal) EM field modes, treated as harmonic oscillators
interaction between charges and EM modes
variables.typical: weak in free space; strong in cavity

equations.context: The 'correct' theory. In free space H_ch-EM is weak (main effect = spontaneous emission); standard quantum chemistry treats only H_ch explicitly and emission perturbatively. In cavities H_EM and H_ch-EM must be treated explicitly.
equations.origin: Theoretical Challenges in Polaritonic Chemistry

equations.name: Repartitioned (cavity) Hamiltonian — starting point of polaritonic chemistry

variables.meaning: the molecule (or system treated in detail) Hamiltonian — electronic + nuclear
combined excitations of the coupled cavity material plus free-space EM modes; if cavity material treated by linear response, diagonalizing H_cav = solving macroscopic Maxwell equations
variables.typical: single or few effective modes with finite linewidth

equations.context: Obtained by regrouping parts of H_ch and H_ch-EM (the cavity material and its interaction with the EM field) together with H_EM into a new 'cavity' Hamiltonian. Caveat: a cavity is really a material system; for plasmonic (strongly subwavelength) nanocavities the coupling is mediated by longitudinal Coulomb interactions (an E·d interaction even in minimal coupling), so the dipole-self-energy term should NOT be included. Describes three DOF types: electronic (r), nuclear (R), photonic (q).
equations.origin: Theoretical Challenges in Polaritonic Chemistry

equations.name: Cavity-mode Hamiltonian (position-space and ladder-operator forms)

variables.meaning: photonic position-space coordinate of the cavity mode
conjugate momentum of the cavity mode

equations.context: Harmonic cavity mode. Position-space form natural for Born-Huang/polaritonic-PES treatment; ladder form natural in quantum optics. Quality factor relations: Q = omega_c/kappa, tau = 1/kappa.
equations.origin: Theoretical Challenges in Polaritonic Chemistry

equations.name: Born-Huang expansion of the polaritonic wavefunction

variables.typical: reaction coordinate in a.u. in figures

variables.meaning: photonic coordinate (fast DOF, grouped with electrons)
nuclear wavepacket amplitude on polaritonic surface k
variables.typical: propagated by MCTDH / grid / surface hopping

variables.meaning: mixed photonic-electronic (polaritonic) eigenstates of the Hamiltonian without nuclear kinetic energy; depend parametrically on R
variables.typical: give polaritonic PES when energies plotted vs R

equations.context: Electronic strong-coupling regime: cavity modes near-resonant with electronic transitions, electron+cavity dynamics fast, so grouped together (slow nuclei separated). Yields polaritonic potential energy surfaces (PoPES). In vibrational strong coupling (VSC) one instead groups photonic+nuclear coordinates {q,R} parametric to electrons -> cavity Born-Oppenheimer (CBO) approximation; CBO works well for VSC on the ground state but breaks down for electronic strong coupling where electron-photon hybridization is essential.
equations.origin: Theoretical Challenges in Polaritonic Chemistry

equations.name: Effective nuclear-photonic (single-mode cavity) Hamiltonian

variables.meaning: photon creation / annihilation operators of the single cavity mode

variables.typical: set by L_c; V_c = A_c L_c

equations.context: Single-mode, single-molecule cavity QED in the long-wavelength (dipole) approximation, Coulomb gauge, after projecting onto the electronic ground state (Born-Oppenheimer); nuclei treated classically. The last two terms are the bilinear light-matter coupling and the dipole self-energy term.

equations.name: Minimal-coupling QED Hamiltonian (Coulomb gauge)

variables.meaning: mass and charge of particle i (electrons and nuclei)
transverse electric and magnetic field operators

equations.context: Nonrelativistic QED starting point in Coulomb gauge; all charged particles couple via longitudinal Coulomb interaction and to the transverse vector potential. Reduced to the effective Hamiltonian above by single-mode + dipole + Born-Oppenheimer approximations.

gradient with respect to position of nucleus j

equations.name: Optical (cavity) force on nucleus j

variables.meaning: optical force on nucleus j from light-matter coupling

variables.units: depends on normalization (sqrt(J)·s units of mode coordinate)

variables.meaning: gradient of the projected dipole moment, from the NEP dipole model
as above (cavity frequency, volume, permittivity, polarization)

equations.context: Single-mode classical-nuclei cavity MD. The total force on nucleus j is F^j = F^j_PES + F^j_c (Eq 1 + Eq 2). The term containing q_c is the bilinear coupling; the (eps_c·mu)^2 term is the dipole self-energy contribution. This is the only light-matter interaction in the model.

equations.name: Cavity mode displacement (history integral over dipole)

variables.meaning: initial cavity displacement; chosen so cavity force is zero at t=0
molecular dipole moment at earlier time t'
initial cavity momentum, explicitly set to zero

equations.context: Self-consistent driven cavity mode; the photon coordinate depends on the full history of the molecular dipole. Initial momentum q_c-dot(0)=0 is enforced; initial state chosen so no electric field (cavity force) exists at t=0.
equations.origin: Machine Learning for Polaritonic Chemistry: Accessing Chemical Kinetics

equations.name: Effective single-molecule coupling strength

variables.meaning: cavity frequency, reduced Planck constant, permittivity, cavity volume

equations.context: Defines the coupling scale used throughout; the dimensionless ratio g0/omega_c is held constant (=1.132 for main results) while scanning omega_c. Note g0 proportional sqrt(omega_c)/sqrt(L_c) since V_c = A_c L_c and omega_c proportional 1/L_c (Fabry-Perot tuning).

variables.meaning: atomic site energy of atom i; total energy U = sum_i U_i

equations.context: NEP machine-learning model architecture: a single-hidden-layer MLP with tanh activation, mapping a per-atom descriptor (with trainable descriptor parameters) to atomic energy. Used for both the PES model and (extended form) the dipole model.

variables.meaning: molecular dipole moment vector (tensorial NEP output)

equations.context: Tensorial extension of the NEP formalism to predict dipole moments; this mu and its gradient feed the optical force (Eq 2) and the cavity displacement (q_c history integral). Note: equation as printed uses outer product (x) notation; the second form collapses it.

transmission coefficient (here used only to compare with experiment)

The expansion parameter scales as 1/sqrt(V_c); only g0/omega_c takes appreciable values. Explains why ML+MD lacks an additional barrier increase (extra suppression) present in QEDFT.

equations.name: Hopfield polariton dispersion (exciton-photon coupling)
equations.context: Polariton (mixed exciton-photon) dispersion near an excitonic resonance; the avoided crossing of the photon line and exciton level is the defining polariton feature. Exact functional form transcribed schematically; the paper derives the dielectric-constant contribution of excitons from which this dispersion follows.
equations.origin: web:History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

equations.name: Jaynes-Cummings Hamiltonian
equations.latex: H = \hbar\omega_c\, a^\dagger a + \frac{1}{2}\hbar\omega_0\, \sigma_z + \hbar g\,(\sigma_+ a + \sigma_- a^\dagger)
equations.context: Rotating-wave approximation, single two-level atom coupled to a single quantized cavity mode. Foundational cavity-QED model. The standard modern operator form is given here; the 1963 paper formulates the equivalent beam-maser problem.
equations.origin: web:History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

equations.name: Vacuum Rabi (normal-mode) splitting at resonance

variables.meaning: Rabi splitting between upper and lower polariton branches

equations.context: On-resonance splitting observed in a semiconductor quantum-well microcavity; strong-coupling regime where Omega_R exceeds cavity and exciton linewidths. The vacuum (n=0) limit is the defining vacuum-Rabi splitting.
equations.origin: web:History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

equations.name: Tavis-Cummings collective coupling (sqrt-N enhancement)
equations.latex: H = \hbar\omega_c a^\dagger a + \sum_{j=1}^{N}\frac{\hbar\omega_0}{2}\sigma_z^{(j)} + \hbar g\sum_{j=1}^{N}\left(\sigma_+^{(j)} a + \sigma_-^{(j)} a^\dagger\right), \qquad \Omega_R^{coll} = 2g\sqrt{N}
equations.context: N identical two-level systems coupled to one quantized mode (Dicke/Tavis-Cummings). Gives the collective sqrt(N) enhancement of the Rabi splitting underlying all collective strong-coupling chemistry.
equations.origin: web:History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

variables.meaning: cavity photon energy at in-plane wavevector k_parallel
background refractive index of the cavity medium
quantized perpendicular (cavity-axis) wavevector of the confined photon mode, k_z = pi M / L_z
variables.typical: set by cavity length L_z, integer mode index M

variables.meaning: in-plane wavevector (set experimentally by emission/incidence angle, k_parallel = (E/hbar c) sin theta)
variables.typical: 0 to a few um^-1 over the angular range probed

equations.where: Carusotto & Ciuti, Rev. Mod. Phys. 85, 299 (2013), Eq. (1); same form in Deng, Haug & Yamamoto Rev. Mod. Phys. 82, 1489 (2010)
equations.context: Planar (1D-confined) Fabry-Perot microcavity, single transverse photon mode with fixed k_z; valid for the lossless cavity photon dispersion.
equations.origin: web:Standard physics of microcavity exciton-polaritons: cavity/exciton dispersions, the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian, lower/upper polariton eigenvalues, detuning, Hopfield coefficients, and canonical numerical values for accurate visualization.

equations.name: Cavity photon parabolic approximation and effective mass
equations.latex: E_{\mathrm{cav}}(k_\parallel) \simeq E_{\mathrm{cav}}^{0} + \frac{\hbar^2 k_\parallel^2}{2 m_{\mathrm{cav}}}, \qquad m_{\mathrm{cav}} = \frac{\hbar n k_z}{c} = \frac{n^2 E_{\mathrm{cav}}^{0}}{c^2}

variables.units: kg (or in units of free-electron mass m_e/m_0)

variables.meaning: cavity photon cutoff energy at k_parallel = 0 (= hbar c k_z / n)

equations.where: Carusotto & Ciuti, Rev. Mod. Phys. 85, 299 (2013), Eqs. (1)-(2); Deng, Sebald, et al. / Deng & Yamamoto PNAS 100, 15318 (2003) define m = hbar n_r k_z / c
equations.context: Small in-plane wavevector expansion (k_parallel << k_z) of the full square-root dispersion; the photon behaves as a 2D massive particle. m_cav is ~4 orders of magnitude lighter than the exciton mass.
equations.origin: web:Standard physics of microcavity exciton-polaritons: cavity/exciton dispersions, the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian, lower/upper polariton eigenvalues, detuning, Hopfield coefficients, and canonical numerical values for accurate visualization.

equations.name: Exciton dispersion (nearly flat)

variables.meaning: exciton translational (center-of-mass) effective mass = m_e* + m_h*
variables.typical: ~0.1-1 m_e (e.g. ~0.5 m_0 in GaAs QWs); ~1e4 x m_cav

equations.where: Carusotto & Ciuti, Rev. Mod. Phys. 85, 299 (2013) (text following Eq. 20); Deng, Haug & Yamamoto Rev. Mod. Phys. 82, 1489 (2010)
equations.context: Because m_exc >> m_cav, over the small k_parallel range relevant to the cavity (a few um^-1) the exciton dispersion is essentially flat compared to the photon parabola; this asymmetry produces the characteristic polariton anticrossing geometry.
equations.origin: web:Standard physics of microcavity exciton-polaritons: cavity/exciton dispersions, the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian, lower/upper polariton eigenvalues, detuning, Hopfield coefficients, and canonical numerical values for accurate visualization.

equations.name: Coupled-oscillator (Hopfield) 2x2 Hamiltonian

variables.meaning: light-matter coupling strength (off-diagonal element); equal to half the vacuum-field Rabi splitting
vacuum-field Rabi splitting (full normal-mode splitting at resonance) = 2V

equations.where: Deveaud (ed.) / 'Fifteen years of microcavity polaritons' arXiv:0811.2502, Sec. on two-coupled-oscillator model, Eq. (1); Carusotto & Ciuti RMP 85, 299 (2013) Eq. (21); standard form e.g. Kavokin & Baumberg, 'Microcavities' (OUP)
equations.context: Linear strong-coupling regime, single exciton and single photon mode, rotating-wave approximation, neglecting damping. Diagonal terms carry the k_parallel-dependence; V is taken k-independent near k_parallel ~ 0. Convention: Rabi splitting = 2V at zero detuning. (Note: some papers, e.g. Deng-Yamamoto PNAS 2003 Eq. 1, write the off-diagonal coupling so that the radicand contains 4*Omega with Omega the coupling; numerically the resonant splitting equals 2 x off-diagonal element in all conventions.)
equations.origin: web:Standard physics of microcavity exciton-polaritons: cavity/exciton dispersions, the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian, lower/upper polariton eigenvalues, detuning, Hopfield coefficients, and canonical numerical values for accurate visualization.

equations.name: Lower / upper polariton eigenvalues

variables.typical: below both bare modes; minimum at k_parallel = 0 for delta<=0

variables.meaning: Rabi splitting; minimum gap between branches at resonance

equations.where: Deng & Yamamoto, PNAS 100, 15318 (2003), Eq. (1) [E_LP form]; Deveaud review arXiv:0811.2502 Eq. (1) [both branches, lossless limit]; Carusotto & Ciuti RMP 85, 299 (2013) Eq. (24)
equations.context: Eigenvalues of the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian. At resonance (E_cav = E_exc) the splitting E_UP - E_LP = 2V = hbar*Omega_R; the branches anticross and never cross (level repulsion). Equivalent compact form: E = (1/2)[E_cav + E_exc +/- sqrt(delta^2 + (hbar Omega_R)^2)].
equations.origin: web:Standard physics of microcavity exciton-polaritons: cavity/exciton dispersions, the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian, lower/upper polariton eigenvalues, detuning, Hopfield coefficients, and canonical numerical values for accurate visualization.

equations.name: Detuning

variables.typical: -10 to +10 meV typical experimental tuning range

equations.where: Deng, Haug & Yamamoto Rev. Mod. Phys. 82, 1489 (2010) (Delta = E_C - E_X); same convention in Carusotto & Ciuti RMP 85, 299 (2013)
equations.context: delta < 0 => photon-like (lower-energy) LP at k=0 with light effective mass; delta > 0 => exciton-like LP; delta = 0 is resonance (50/50 mixing at k=0). Sign convention E_cav - E_exc is the most common; some authors use E_exc - E_cav, flipping the sign in the Hopfield formula.
equations.origin: web:Standard physics of microcavity exciton-polaritons: cavity/exciton dispersions, the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian, lower/upper polariton eigenvalues, detuning, Hopfield coefficients, and canonical numerical values for accurate visualization.

equations.name: Hopfield coefficients (exciton and photon fractions)

variables.meaning: excitonic (matter) fraction of the lower polariton
variables.typical: 0.5 at delta=0; ->1 for large positive delta

variables.meaning: photonic (light) fraction of the lower polariton
variables.typical: 0.5 at delta=0; ->1 for large negative delta

variables.meaning: detuning E_cav - E_exc at the given k_parallel

equations.where: Hopfield, Phys. Rev. 112, 1555 (1958) (original definition); explicit detuning form as in Deng, Haug & Yamamoto RMP 82, 1489 (2010) and Kavokin & Baumberg 'Microcavities'; equivalent |X|^2 = (1/2)(1 + Delta/sqrt(Delta^2 + 4|V|^2)) widely quoted
equations.context: Eigenvector weights of the 2x2 Hamiltonian; give the squared amplitudes of the exciton and photon components in the lower polariton (upper polariton has X and C swapped). With this sign convention (delta = E_cav - E_exc), positive detuning makes the LP more excitonic. At resonance delta=0 the LP is exactly 50% exciton / 50% photon. These coefficients set the effective mass, interaction strength, lifetime, and emission of each branch as functions of k_parallel and detuning.
equations.origin: web:Standard physics of microcavity exciton-polaritons: cavity/exciton dispersions, the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian, lower/upper polariton eigenvalues, detuning, Hopfield coefficients, and canonical numerical values for accurate visualization.

equations.name: Jaynes-Cummings Hamiltonian (single emitter, RWA)

variables.meaning: vacuum Rabi frequency / single-emitter light-matter coupling
Pauli inversion and raising/lowering operators of the two-level emitter; sigma_- = sigma_+^dagger = sigma_x - i sigma_y

equations.context: Single two-level system coupled to one cavity mode under the rotating-wave approximation (RWA); valid in weak-to-strong coupling where counter-rotating terms are negligible (g << omega_c, omega_eg). Conserves excitation number.
equations.origin: web:Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

equations.name: Vacuum Rabi splitting (resonant single-photon doublet)

variables.meaning: excitation-number quantum number of the JC doublet
variables.typical: 1 for the single-photon vacuum Rabi splitting

equations.context: At zero cavity-emitter detuning (Delta = omega_c - omega_eg = 0). The n=1 doublet gives the experimentally measured vacuum Rabi splitting 2g. The sqrt(n) scaling (Jaynes-Cummings ladder anharmonicity) underlies photon blockade. Off resonance, omega_{n,+}-omega_{n,-} = sqrt(Delta^2 + 4 Omega_R^2 n).
equations.origin: web:Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

equations.name: Quantum Rabi model (no RWA; counter-rotating terms)

variables.meaning: Pauli-x of the emitter; sigma_x = sigma_+ + sigma_-, generates flip-flop AND counter-rotating (a-dagger sigma_+ , a sigma_-) terms

variables.typical: becomes comparable to omega_c in ultrastrong coupling (USC), Omega_R/omega_c >~ 0.1

equations.context: Recovered by removing the RWA from the JCM. Breaks excitation-number conservation (only Z2 parity remains), so the ground state contains virtual photons. Needed in the ultrastrong/deep-strong coupling regimes.
equations.origin: web:Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

~(lambda/2)^3 for Fabry-Perot; down to <~10^-6 lambda^3 (nm^3) for plasmonic nanogaps

variables.meaning: background relative permittivity of the medium filling the mode

equations.context: Single electric dipole coupled to the cavity vacuum field. g scales as 1/sqrt(V): shrinking the mode volume (plasmonic picocavities) is the route to single-molecule strong coupling. Assumes dipole aligned with field and emitter inside the mode volume.

single-emitter coupling of TLS j to the mode
raising/lowering and inversion operators of emitter j
number of emitters collectively coupled to the mode

equations.name: Collective bright (superradiant) state and collective coupling

variables.meaning: symmetric single-excitation (bright/superradiant) collective state, zero photons
collective vacuum Rabi splitting (upper-lower polariton splitting at resonance)
variables.typical: tens of meV to ~1 eV (excitonic); few cm^-1 to meV (vibrational)

equations.where: Mandal, Taylor, Weight, Koessler, Li, Huo, Chem. Rev. 2023, 123, 9786 (DOI 10.1021/acs.chemrev.2c00855), Eq. (13); sqrt(N) per arXiv:2110.14174 / arXiv:2402.09885 Eq. (4)
equations.context: Single-excitation manifold of N identical emitters. Only the totally symmetric combination couples to the photon, with enhanced coupling g_N = g sqrt(N); the collective Rabi splitting therefore scales as sqrt(N) (equivalently sqrt(molecular density)).
equations.origin: web:Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

equations.name: Upper / lower polariton states (collective)

variables.meaning: all-ground-state molecules with one cavity photon
mixing (Hopfield) angle; tan(2 theta_N) = 2 g_N / Delta, theta_N = pi/4 at resonance (50:50 light-matter)

equations.where: Mandal et al., Chem. Rev. 2023 (DOI 10.1021/acs.chemrev.2c00855), Eqs. (14a)-(14b)
equations.context: Hybridization of the bright collective matter state with the single-photon state yields two polaritons split by the collective vacuum Rabi splitting 2 g_N (at resonance). These are the only optically bright (absorbing) states.
equations.origin: web:Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

equations.name: Dark states (N-1 uncoupled collective states)

variables.meaning: expansion coefficients of dark state alpha over single-molecule excitations, constrained to sum to zero
number of degenerate dark states (single-excitation manifold)

equations.where: Mandal et al., Chem. Rev. 2023 (DOI 10.1021/acs.chemrev.2c00855), Eq. (18)
equations.context: The N-1 combinations orthogonal to the bright state have zero net transition dipole and do not couple to the cavity field; they remain (in the bare TC model) at the original uncoupled molecular energy. Disorder/dephasing weakly couples them to the polaritons (a central theme in polariton chemistry).
equations.origin: web:Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

equations.name: Dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz (length-gauge) Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED

variables.meaning: total molecular electric-dipole operator (sum over all charges)
per-photon field amplitude vector, E_0 = e_hat sqrt(hbar omega_c / (2 eps0 V))

equations.where: Mandal, Taylor, Weight, Koessler, Li, Huo, Chem. Rev. 2023, 123, 9786 (DOI 10.1021/acs.chemrev.2c00855), Eq. (56) and Eq. (3) for E_0/g_c
equations.context: Exact, gauge-invariant non-relativistic light-matter Hamiltonian (dipole/length gauge, long-wavelength dipole approximation, single mode shown; generalizes to many modes/molecules). The final term is the dipole self-energy (DSE), essential for gauge invariance, a bounded-below spectrum, and correct strong/ultrastrong-coupling results. Expanding mu in a two-level basis and applying the RWA recovers the JC/Tavis-Cummings models.
equations.origin: web:Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

equations.name: Marcus / Fermi-golden-rule ET rate (state-to-state)
equations.latex: K(V,E_a)=\sqrt{\frac{\pi}{E_r k_B T}}\,\frac{|V|^2}{2\hbar}\,\exp\!\left(-\frac{E_a^2}{4 k_B T E_r}\right),\qquad E_a=E_{fi}+E_r,\qquad E_r=\frac{\lambda_v^2}{2\omega_v^2}

equations.where: Sharma & Chen, J. Chem. Phys. 161, 104102 (2024), Eqs. (44a),(44b)
equations.context: Derived from the barrier-less (resonance) condition Omega_+^{(I)}-hbar*omega_c=0 (Omega_0^{(II)}+hbar*omega_c=0), i.e. the collective polariton PES becomes barrier-less. Using Omega_+^{(I)}|_{Delta=0}=sqrt((N-1)|T_01|^2)=hbar*omega_c gives N-1=(hbar*omega_c)^2/|T_01|^2; the paper writes Eq.(44) as 1+hbar*omega_c/|T_01|^2 (consistent only if |T_01|^2 is taken in units making the ratio dimensionless / a unit convention in the paper). CROSS-CHECK on the task's note: published form is hbar*omega_c/|T_01|^2, and because resonance fixes hbar*omega_c = E_AD, the gap E_AD enters as the numerator; it is NOT hbar*omega_c*E_AD/|T_01|^2.

equations.name: Collective light-matter (Tavis-Cummings-type) Hamiltonian for VSC
equations.latex: H = \hbar\omega_c a_0^\dagger a_0 + \hbar\sum_{i=1}^{N}\omega_i a_i^\dagger a_i + \hbar\sum_{i=1}^{N} g_i\,(a_i^\dagger a_0 + \mathrm{h.c.})

variables.meaning: number of molecules coupled to the mode

equations.where: Du & Yuen-Zhou, Phys. Rev. Lett. 128, 096001 (2022), arXiv:2104.07214 (exact form from the PDF text)
equations.context: Collective strong coupling of N localized vibrations to one cavity mode; yields 2 polaritons + (N-1) dark modes. Assumes single-excitation/harmonic regime and identical coupling.
equations.origin: web:The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

equations.name: Per-molecule coupling scales as 1/sqrt(N) (constant-density Hamiltonian)

variables.meaning: collective coupling parameter (fixed at constant molecular density)
number of molecules; per-molecule prefactor is g/sqrt(N)
transition dipole operator of molecule i

equations.where: Lindoy, Mandal & Reichman, Nanophotonics 13, 2617 (2024), doi:10.1515/nanoph-2024-0026 (Eq. 9), arXiv:2403.03951
equations.context: At fixed density the individual molecule-cavity coupling ~ g/sqrt(N) -> 0 as N->infinity, so any single reacting molecule feels a vanishing perturbation. This is the quantitative heart of the 'large N problem.'
equations.origin: web:The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

equations.name: Collective Rabi splitting scales as sqrt(N)

variables.meaning: vacuum Rabi splitting (the measured polariton energy gap)
variables.typical: ~20-200 cm^-1 for VSC; up to ~10% of vibration freq

equations.where: Lindoy, Mandal & Reichman, Nanophotonics 13, 2617 (2024); also general VSC literature (e.g. Galego et al.)
equations.context: The experimentally observed quantity (Rabi splitting) is collectively enhanced by sqrt(N), but this enhancement is shared among ~N molecules; it does NOT mean any single molecule is strongly perturbed. The tension between Omega_R ∝ sqrt(N) (large, measurable) and per-molecule coupling ∝ 1/sqrt(N) (negligible) is the crux of the controversy.
equations.origin: web:The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

equations.name: Polariton frequencies and dark-mode count under collective VSC

variables.meaning: number of optically dark modes parked at ~\omega_v

equations.where: Du & Yuen-Zhou, Phys. Rev. Lett. 128, 096001 (2022), arXiv:2104.07214
equations.context: Only 2 of N+1 hybrid modes are bright polaritons; the remaining N-1 dark modes stay near the bare frequency. Entropically, reactivity changes attributed only to the 2 polaritons look 'entropically unlikely' (hence the dark-state and disorder/semilocalization arguments).
equations.origin: web:The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

equations.where: Bloch sphere, Wikipedia (and Nielsen & Chuang convention)
equations.context: Two-level system. Cartesian axes = <σ_x>, <σ_y>, <σ_z> (Bloch vector). Density matrix rho = (I + r·σ)/2, |r|<=1; surface (|r|=1) = pure states, interior = mixed states. North |0> / south |1> is the standard but arbitrary convention.

driving force = vertical offset of product minimum (ΔE in cavity papers)

equations.context: Two intersecting parabolas of EQUAL curvature; reorganization energy lambda = curvature x (displacement)^2 = vertical gap at reactant geometry. Diagram axes: free energy G vs reaction coordinate q.

equations.context: Normal regime when -ΔG°<λ; barrierless when -ΔG°=λ; inverted regime when -ΔG°>λ (barrier reappears). The vertex of the parabola crossing is the barrier marked on the diagram.

equations.where: Tokmakoff / LibreTexts 19.4; Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo, Ribeiro, Yuen-Zhou, Nat. Commun. 10 (2019) 4685, Eq. 1 (MLJ form with Huang-Rhys sum)
equations.context: Nonadiabatic (weak-coupling) limit. The MLJ generalization adds a Franck-Condon-weighted sum over high-frequency vibrational quanta v. Cavity reshapes the product surfaces into LP/UP/dark channels each with its own ΔG‡.

variables.meaning: collective vacuum Rabi splitting, scales as sqrt(N)

equations.where: Bhatt et al. review PMC10941193; Mandal, Huo et al. 'Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry' Chem. Rev. 123 (2023) 9786; Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo et al. Nat. Commun. 10 (2019) 4685, Eq. 4 (omega_±(N))
equations.context: N two-level emitters + 1 cavity mode. Two bright polaritons (LP, UP) plus (N-1) DARK states at the bare molecular energy omega_0. The sqrt(N) enhancement is why ensembles reach strong coupling. Drawn on the energy-level/Jablonski diagram.
