facts.claim: ORIGIN (phonon-polaritons): Kirill B. Tolpygo (1950, quantum approach) and independently Huang Kun (1951, classical approach) first obtained the coupled photon-phonon (later 'phonon-polariton') dispersion in ionic crystals.
facts.source: Wikipedia 'Polariton' (citing Tolpygo 1950, Huang 1951), https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Polariton
facts.facet: History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

facts.claim: The term 'polariton' was proposed by John Hopfield; S. I. Pekar's earlier Soviet-literature name was 'light-exciton', but Hopfield's term was adopted.
facts.source: Wikipedia 'Polariton', https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Polariton
facts.facet: History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

facts.claim: EXCITON-POLARITON THEORY: John J. Hopfield, 'Theory of the Contribution of Excitons to the Complex Dielectric Constant of Crystals,' Phys. Rev. 112, 1555 (1958), gave the quantum theory in which crystal eigenstates are mixtures of photons and excitons (polaritons).
facts.source: Hopfield, Phys. Rev. 112, 1555 (1958), https://doi.org/10.1103/PhysRev.112.1555
facts.facet: History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

facts.claim: SUPERRADIANCE: R. H. Dicke (Princeton), 'Coherence in Spontaneous Radiation Processes,' Phys. Rev. 93, 99-110 (1954), introduced collective (super-radiant) spontaneous emission by treating a radiating gas as a single quantum system.
facts.source: Dicke, Phys. Rev. 93, 99 (1954), https://doi.org/10.1103/PhysRev.93.99
facts.facet: History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

facts.claim: JAYNES-CUMMINGS MODEL: E. T. Jaynes & F. W. Cummings, 'Comparison of Quantum and Semiclassical Radiation Theories with Application to the Beam Maser,' Proc. IEEE 51, 89-109 (1963), established the single-atom single-mode quantized light-matter model.
facts.source: Jaynes & Cummings, Proc. IEEE 51, 89 (1963), https://doi.org/10.1109/PROC.1963.1664
facts.facet: History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

facts.claim: TAVIS-CUMMINGS MODEL: Michael Tavis & Frederick W. Cummings, 'Exact Solution for an N-Molecule-Radiation-Field Hamiltonian,' Phys. Rev. 170, 379-384 (1968), generalized Jaynes-Cummings to N emitters, giving the sqrt(N) collective coupling.
facts.source: Tavis & Cummings, Phys. Rev. 170, 379 (1968), https://doi.org/10.1103/PhysRev.170.379
facts.facet: History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

facts.claim: MICROCAVITY STRONG COUPLING: C. Weisbuch, M. Nishioka, A. Ishikawa & Y. Arakawa, 'Observation of the coupled exciton-photon mode splitting in a semiconductor quantum microcavity,' PRL 69, 3314-3317 (1992, Dec 7), first observed vacuum-Rabi/normal-mode splitting of quantum-well excitons — birth of microcavity polaritons.
facts.source: Weisbuch et al., PRL 69, 3314 (1992), https://doi.org/10.1103/PhysRevLett.69.3314
facts.facet: History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

facts.claim: POLARITON LASER PROPOSAL: A. Imamoglu, R. J. Ram, S. Pau & Y. Yamamoto, 'Nonequilibrium condensates and lasers without inversion: Exciton-polariton lasers,' Phys. Rev. A 53, 4250-4253 (1996), proposed coherent light from polariton condensation (inversionless lasing).
facts.source: Imamoglu et al., Phys. Rev. A 53, 4250 (1996), referenced via Wikipedia 'Polariton laser', https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Polariton_laser
facts.facet: History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

facts.claim: POLARITON vs PHOTON LASING: H. Deng, G. Weihs, D. Snoke, J. Bloch & Y. Yamamoto, 'Polariton lasing vs. photon lasing in a semiconductor microcavity,' PNAS 100, 15318-15323 (2003), distinguished the two regimes experimentally.
facts.source: Deng et al., PNAS 100, 15318 (2003), referenced via search results
facts.facet: History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

facts.claim: POLARITON BEC: J. Kasprzak, M. Richard, S. Kundermann, A. Baas et al., 'Bose-Einstein condensation of exciton polaritons,' Nature 443, 409-414 (28 Sep 2006), reported spontaneous macroscopic quantum coherence (BEC) of polaritons in a CdTe microcavity.
facts.source: Kasprzak et al., Nature 443, 409 (2006), https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17006506/
facts.facet: History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

facts.claim: ROOM-TEMPERATURE POLARITON LASING: Christopoulos et al., 'Room-Temperature Polariton Lasing in Semiconductor Microcavities,' PRL 98, 126405 (2007), demonstrated polariton lasing at room temperature (GaN microcavity).
facts.source: Christopoulos et al., PRL 98, 126405 (2007), referenced via search results
facts.facet: History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

facts.claim: BIRTH OF POLARITONIC CHEMISTRY: J. A. Hutchison, T. Schwartz, C. Genet, E. Devaux & T. W. Ebbesen (Strasbourg), 'Modifying Chemical Landscapes by Coupling to Vacuum Fields,' Angew. Chem. Int. Ed. 51, 1592-1596 (2012), showed an electronic (photochemical) reaction altered by strong coupling to the cavity vacuum field.
facts.source: Hutchison et al., Angew. Chem. Int. Ed. 51, 1592 (2012), https://doi.org/10.1002/anie.201107033
facts.facet: History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

facts.claim: VIBRATIONAL STRONG COUPLING CHEMISTRY: A. Thomas et al. (Ebbesen group), 'Ground-State Chemical Reactivity under Vibrational Coupling to the Vacuum Electromagnetic Field,' Angew. Chem. Int. Ed. 55, 11462-11466 (2016), showed a ground-state deprotection rate slowed up to 5.5x when the Si-C stretch was vibrationally strongly coupled.
facts.source: Thomas et al., Angew. Chem. Int. Ed. 55, 11462 (2016), https://doi.org/10.1002/anie.201605504
facts.facet: History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

facts.claim: VSC SITE-SELECTIVITY: A. Thomas et al. (Ebbesen group), 'Tilting a ground-state reactivity landscape by vibrational strong coupling,' Science 363, 615-619 (2019), showed VSC of distinct vibrational modes can steer product selectivity (Si-C vs Si-O cleavage).
facts.source: Thomas et al., Science 363, 615 (2019), https://doi.org/10.1126/science.aau7742
facts.facet: History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

facts.claim: THEORY (molecular structure under strong coupling): J. Galego, F. J. Garcia-Vidal & J. Feist, 'Cavity-Induced Modifications of Molecular Structure in the Strong-Coupling Regime,' Phys. Rev. X 5, 041022 (2015), built a first-principles model with full electronic+nuclear degrees of freedom beyond the two-level picture.
facts.source: Galego, Garcia-Vidal & Feist, PRX 5, 041022 (2015), https://doi.org/10.1103/PhysRevX.5.041022
facts.facet: History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

facts.claim: THEORY (QEDFT): J. Flick, M. Ruggenthaler, H. Appel & A. Rubio, 'Atoms and molecules in cavities, from weak to strong coupling in quantum-electrodynamics (QED) chemistry,' PNAS 114, 3026-3034 (2017), established ab initio QED density-functional methods for cavity chemistry.
facts.source: Flick, Ruggenthaler, Appel & Rubio, PNAS 114, 3026 (2017), https://www.pnas.org/doi/10.1073/pnas.1615509114
facts.facet: History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

facts.claim: THEORY (review): A. Mandal, M. Taylor, B. Weight, E. Koessler, X. Li & P. Huo, 'Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics,' Chem. Rev. 123, 9786-9879 (2023), is the comprehensive theory review of the field.
facts.source: Mandal et al., Chem. Rev. 123, 9786 (2023), https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10450711/
facts.facet: History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

facts.claim: CAVITY-MODIFIED ELECTRON TRANSFER / HTC: S. K. Sharma & H.-T. Chen (Univ. of Notre Dame), 'Unraveling Abnormal Collective Effects via the Non-Monotonic Number Dependence of Electron Transfer in Confined Electromagnetic Fields,' J. Chem. Phys. 161, 104109 (2024), DOI 10.1063/5.0225434, studies collective effects in nonadiabatic electron transfer in a cavity (Holstein-Tavis-Cummings-type framework).
facts.facet: History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

facts.claim: The non-equilibrium Fermi-golden-rule rate theory for the Holstein-Tavis-Cummings Hamiltonian (a universal model for many molecules collectively coupled to a cavity) underlies modern cavity electron/energy-transfer rate theory.
facts.source: AIP J. Chem. Phys. polariton rate-theory articles, https://pubs.aip.org/aip/jcp/article/161/10/104109/3312358/
facts.facet: History & timeline of polariton and cavity-QED physics (verified history tree)

facts.claim: Microcavity exciton-polaritons are the eigenmodes of strong (reversible) coupling between a confined cavity photon and a quantum-well exciton; the regime is signaled by an anticrossing of the photon and exciton modes whose minimum splitting equals the vacuum-field Rabi splitting.
facts.source: Deveaud (ed.) review, 'Fifteen years of microcavity polaritons', arXiv:0811.2502 (intro and two-coupled-oscillator section)
facts.facet: Standard physics of microcavity exciton-polaritons: cavity/exciton dispersions, the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian, lower/upper polariton eigenvalues, detuning, Hopfield coefficients, and canonical numerical values for accurate visualization.

facts.claim: The cavity photon in-plane dispersion is E_cav(k_parallel) = (hbar c / n) sqrt(k_z^2 + k_parallel^2), which for k_parallel << k_z reduces to a parabola E_cav^0 + hbar^2 k_parallel^2 / (2 m_cav).
facts.source: Carusotto & Ciuti, Rev. Mod. Phys. 85, 299 (2013), Eq. (1)
facts.facet: Standard physics of microcavity exciton-polaritons: cavity/exciton dispersions, the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian, lower/upper polariton eigenvalues, detuning, Hopfield coefficients, and canonical numerical values for accurate visualization.

facts.claim: The effective cavity photon mass is m_cav = hbar n k_z / c (equivalently n^2 E_cav^0 / c^2), giving a value on the order of 1e-5 to 1e-4 free-electron masses, about four orders of magnitude lighter than the exciton mass.
facts.source: Carusotto & Ciuti, Rev. Mod. Phys. 85, 299 (2013), Eq. (2); Deng & Yamamoto, PNAS 100, 15318 (2003)
facts.facet: Standard physics of microcavity exciton-polaritons: cavity/exciton dispersions, the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian, lower/upper polariton eigenvalues, detuning, Hopfield coefficients, and canonical numerical values for accurate visualization.

facts.claim: A specific measured cavity photon effective mass of 3.6e-5 m_0 and a Rabi splitting of hbar*Omega = 15.9 +/- 0.1 meV are reported for a representative II-VI (CdTe) microcavity used in polariton condensation studies.
facts.source: Deng, Haug & Yamamoto, Rev. Mod. Phys. 82, 1489 (2010) (via PMC-hosted polariton condensate study citing these parameters)
facts.facet: Standard physics of microcavity exciton-polaritons: cavity/exciton dispersions, the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian, lower/upper polariton eigenvalues, detuning, Hopfield coefficients, and canonical numerical values for accurate visualization.

facts.claim: The exciton dispersion is essentially flat on the scale of the photon dispersion because the exciton translational mass (~0.1-1 m_e) is roughly 1e4 times the photon mass.
facts.source: Deng & Yamamoto, PNAS 100, 15318 (2003) ('cavity photons have an effective mass ... four orders of magnitude lighter than the exciton effective mass')
facts.facet: Standard physics of microcavity exciton-polaritons: cavity/exciton dispersions, the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian, lower/upper polariton eigenvalues, detuning, Hopfield coefficients, and canonical numerical values for accurate visualization.

facts.claim: The strong-coupling system is described by a 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian with cavity and exciton energies on the diagonal and an off-diagonal coupling V that is half the Rabi splitting (Rabi splitting = 2V at resonance).
facts.source: Deveaud review, arXiv:0811.2502, two-coupled-oscillator model (Eq. 1); Kavokin & Baumberg, 'Microcavities' (OUP, 2nd ed. 2017)
facts.facet: Standard physics of microcavity exciton-polaritons: cavity/exciton dispersions, the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian, lower/upper polariton eigenvalues, detuning, Hopfield coefficients, and canonical numerical values for accurate visualization.

facts.claim: Lower/upper polariton eigenvalues are E_LP/UP = (1/2)[E_cav + E_exc +/- sqrt((E_cav - E_exc)^2 + (2V)^2)]; the lossless eigenfrequency form omega = (1/2)(omega_x + omega_c) +/- (1/2) sqrt(Omega_R^2 + (omega_x - omega_c)^2) is given explicitly in the Deveaud review.
facts.source: Deveaud review arXiv:0811.2502, Eq. (1); Deng & Yamamoto PNAS 100, 15318 (2003), Eq. (1)
facts.facet: Standard physics of microcavity exciton-polaritons: cavity/exciton dispersions, the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian, lower/upper polariton eigenvalues, detuning, Hopfield coefficients, and canonical numerical values for accurate visualization.

facts.claim: The detuning is defined as delta (Delta) = E_cav - E_exc (E_C - E_X); the two polariton branches anticross as detuning is varied and never cross.
facts.source: Deng, Haug & Yamamoto, Rev. Mod. Phys. 82, 1489 (2010) (Delta = E_C - E_X)
facts.facet: Standard physics of microcavity exciton-polaritons: cavity/exciton dispersions, the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian, lower/upper polariton eigenvalues, detuning, Hopfield coefficients, and canonical numerical values for accurate visualization.

facts.claim: The Hopfield coefficients give the excitonic fraction |X|^2 = (1/2)(1 + delta/sqrt(delta^2 + (2V)^2)) and photonic fraction |C|^2 = 1 - |X|^2; at zero detuning the polariton is exactly 50% exciton and 50% photon.
facts.source: Hopfield, Phys. Rev. 112, 1555 (1958); Deng & Yamamoto PNAS 100, 15318 (2003) ('exciton fraction of the k_parallel=0 polariton x(0) = 1/2'); Kavokin & Baumberg 'Microcavities'
facts.facet: Standard physics of microcavity exciton-polaritons: cavity/exciton dispersions, the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian, lower/upper polariton eigenvalues, detuning, Hopfield coefficients, and canonical numerical values for accurate visualization.

facts.claim: Typical vacuum-field Rabi splittings are about 4-16 meV in GaAs/III-V microcavities (up to ~16 meV anticrossing quoted for GaAs), about 10-26 meV in II-VI (CdTe), and tens to over 100 meV in wide-gap, organic, and perovskite systems.
facts.source: Deveaud review arXiv:0811.2502 ('as large as 16 meV in bulk GaAs'); Carusotto & Ciuti RMP 85, 299 (2013) ('Omega_R ~ 10 meV for III-V, ~20 meV for II-VI')
facts.facet: Standard physics of microcavity exciton-polaritons: cavity/exciton dispersions, the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian, lower/upper polariton eigenvalues, detuning, Hopfield coefficients, and canonical numerical values for accurate visualization.

facts.claim: Because the lower polariton at small k_parallel is strongly photon-like, its effective mass near k=0 is about twice the photon mass (~1e-4 m_e), four orders of magnitude lighter than the exciton mass, which is the origin of the very steep LP dispersion bottom.
facts.source: Deng & Yamamoto, PNAS 100, 15318 (2003) ('effective mass m_pol twice the photon mass, four orders of magnitude lighter than the exciton mass')
facts.facet: Standard physics of microcavity exciton-polaritons: cavity/exciton dispersions, the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian, lower/upper polariton eigenvalues, detuning, Hopfield coefficients, and canonical numerical values for accurate visualization.

facts.claim: Including phenomenological damping, the complex eigenfrequencies are omega = (1/2)(omega_x + omega_c) - (i/2)(gamma_x + gamma_c) +/- (1/2) sqrt(Omega_R^2 + (omega_x - omega_c - i(gamma_x - gamma_c))^2), reducing to equal LP/UP linewidths (average of gamma_x, gamma_c) at zero detuning.
facts.source: Deveaud review arXiv:0811.2502, Eq. (1)
facts.facet: Standard physics of microcavity exciton-polaritons: cavity/exciton dispersions, the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian, lower/upper polariton eigenvalues, detuning, Hopfield coefficients, and canonical numerical values for accurate visualization.

facts.claim: The Jaynes-Cummings model describes one two-level system coupled to a single quantized cavity mode in the RWA; the interaction is flip-flop (a sigma_+ + a-dagger sigma_-), which conserves the total excitation number N_hat = a-dagger a + (sigma_z+1)/2 (U(1) symmetry).
facts.source: arXiv:2403.02402 (Le Boite, De Liberato), Eqs. (1)-(2)
facts.facet: Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

facts.claim: Because excitation number is conserved, the JCM block-diagonalizes into 2x2 blocks spanned by {|n,g>, |n-1,e>}; the dressed-state eigenvalues are omega_{n,+/-} = -omega_eg/2 + omega_c n - Delta/2 +/- sqrt(Delta^2/4 + Omega_R^2 n), with detuning Delta = omega_c - omega_eg.
facts.facet: Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

facts.claim: At resonance the intra-doublet splitting is 2 Omega_R sqrt(n); the n=1 case gives the single-photon vacuum Rabi splitting of 2 Omega_R = 2g. This sqrt(n) anharmonicity (Jaynes-Cummings ladder) enables photon blockade.
facts.facet: Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

facts.claim: Removing the RWA gives the quantum Rabi model H_R = hbar omega_c a-dagger a + (hbar omega_eg/2) sigma_z + hbar Omega_R (a+a-dagger) sigma_x, whose counter-rotating terms (a-dagger sigma_+, a sigma_-) break excitation-number conservation (only Z2 parity survives) and put virtual photons in the ground state; they matter in the ultrastrong-coupling regime.
facts.source: arXiv:2403.02402, Eq. (11) and surrounding text
facts.facet: Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

facts.claim: The single-emitter coupling energy is hbar g = mu . E_vac, with RMS vacuum field |E_vac| = sqrt(hbar omega_0 / (2 V_m eps0 eps_host)); hence g = (mu/hbar) sqrt(hbar omega_c / (2 eps0 V)), so g scales as 1/sqrt(V).
facts.source: Rider et al., arXiv:2402.09885, Eqs. (3)-(4)
facts.facet: Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

facts.claim: For N dipoles the interaction scales as sqrt(N): g_N = (1/hbar) sqrt(N) mu sqrt(hbar omega_0 / (2 V_m eps0 eps_host)), and the (collective) vacuum Rabi splitting is Omega_R = omega_+ - omega_- = 2 g_N.
facts.source: Rider et al., arXiv:2402.09885, Eqs. (1) and (4); definition Omega_R = 2 g_N
facts.facet: Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

facts.claim: The molecular strong-coupling condition is Omega_R = 2 g_N > (Gamma + K)/2, i.e. the Rabi splitting must exceed the mean of the molecular (Gamma) and cavity (K) linewidths.
facts.source: Rider et al., arXiv:2402.09885, Eq. (1)
facts.facet: Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

facts.claim: The Tavis-Cummings Hamiltonian for N identical emitters in one mode (RWA) is H_TC = omega_r a-dagger a + sum_j [ (omega_j/2) sigma_z,j + Gamma_j (a-dagger sigma_-,j + sigma_+,j a) ]; it reduces to the JCM for N=1 and exhibits sqrt(N) scaling of the collective coupling, observable as the vacuum Rabi splitting.
facts.source: Dong, Zhang, Wu, Wu, arXiv:2110.14174, Eq. (5) and Sec. III
facts.facet: Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

facts.claim: In the single-excitation sector only the symmetric (bright/superradiant) state |B,0> = (1/sqrt N) sum_J |e_J> couples to the photon; hybridization yields upper and lower polaritons |+/-> = cos/sin theta_N |B,0> +/- sin/cos theta_N |G,1>, with 50:50 light-matter character (theta_N = pi/4) at resonance.
facts.source: Mandal et al., Chem. Rev. 2023 (DOI 10.1021/acs.chemrev.2c00855), Eqs. (13)-(14)
facts.facet: Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

facts.claim: The remaining N-1 collective combinations are degenerate 'dark' states with zero net transition dipole (coefficients sum to zero) that do not couple to the cavity field and remain at the bare molecular energy; line broadening/dephasing can weakly couple them to the polaritons.
facts.source: Mandal et al., Chem. Rev. 2023 (DOI 10.1021/acs.chemrev.2c00855), Eq. (18); Antoniou et al., J. Chem. Phys. 159, 044306 (2023), DOI 10.1063/5.0155302
facts.facet: Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

facts.claim: The Dicke model is the multi-emitter generalization keeping counter-rotating terms: H_I ∝ Omega_R (a+a-dagger) sum_i sigma_x^i; in the dilute (bosonized) limit, with collective operator b = (1/sqrt N) sum_i sigma_-^i, it gives H_I ≈ sqrt(N) Omega_R (a+a-dagger)(b+b-dagger), i.e. a sqrt(N)-enhanced vacuum Rabi frequency.
facts.source: arXiv:2403.02402, Eq. (18) and surrounding text
facts.facet: Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

facts.claim: The rigorous molecular-cavity-QED Hamiltonian is the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz form H_PF = H_M + hbar omega_c (a-dagger a + 1/2) - mu . E_0 q_c + (1/(2 hbar omega_c))(mu . E_0)^2; the final dipole-self-energy term is required for gauge invariance and a bounded-below spectrum.
facts.source: Mandal et al., Chem. Rev. 2023 (DOI 10.1021/acs.chemrev.2c00855), Eq. (56)
facts.facet: Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

facts.claim: Single-molecule strong coupling has been demonstrated in plasmonic nanoparticle-on-mirror picocavities, with a measured vacuum Rabi splitting of g ≈ 380 meV for a single methylene-blue molecule (FDTD-confirmed), enabled by an extremely small mode volume.
facts.source: Chikkaraddy et al., Nature 535, 127 (2016), DOI 10.1038/nature17974 (quoted in arXiv:2403.02402)
facts.facet: Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

facts.claim: Representative molecular collective couplings from routine optical data: g_N ≈ 0.31 eV for a Nile-Red-doped polymer brush (40 nm) and g_N ≈ 0.25 eV for a TDBC J-aggregate film (transition at 2.14 eV), placing excitonic Rabi splittings in the tenths-of-an-eV range.
facts.source: Rider et al., arXiv:2402.09885, worked examples (Sec. on Nile Red and TDBC)
facts.facet: Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

facts.claim: In the single-excitation TC model the N-1 dark states are degenerate, antisymmetric under emitter permutation, and contain the single-excitation subradiant states as a subclass; bright states live in the controllable/observable subspace, dark states in the uncontrollable/unobservable subspace.
facts.source: Dong, Zhang, Wu, Wu, arXiv:2110.14174, Sec. I and Sec. IV
facts.facet: Jaynes-Cummings, Tavis-Cummings, and Dicke models for collective light-matter coupling (cavity QED): exact Hamiltonians, vacuum Rabi splitting, sqrt(N) collective enhancement, single-molecule coupling vs mode volume, bright polaritons vs dark states, and the dipole-gauge Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian of molecular cavity QED.

facts.claim: The paper is by Shravan Kumar Sharma and Hsing-Ta Chen (Univ. of Notre Dame), titled 'Unraveling Abnormal Collective Effects via the Non-Monotonic Number Dependence of Electron Transfer in Confined Electromagnetic Fields,' published in J. Chem. Phys. 161, 104102 (2024), DOI 10.1063/5.0225434; preprint arXiv:2406.17101.
facts.source: arXiv:2406.17101 abstract page (https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.17101); J. Chem. Phys. 161, 104102 (2024), https://doi.org/10.1063/5.0225434
facts.facet: Holstein-Tavis-Cummings model and cavity-modified electron transfer / Marcus theory (cross-check of Sharma & Chen 2024)

facts.claim: The model is explicitly the Holstein-Tavis-Cummings (HTC) model: electronic two-level donor/acceptor states, a Holstein vibrational primary mode coupled to the acceptor, and a single Tavis-Cummings cavity mode collectively coupled to all N molecules.
facts.source: Sharma & Chen 2024, Sec. II and Eqs. (1)-(10) (arXiv:2406.17101 full text)
facts.facet: Holstein-Tavis-Cummings model and cavity-modified electron transfer / Marcus theory (cross-check of Sharma & Chen 2024)

facts.claim: The cavity-photon-mediated effective ET couplings are T_01 = hbar*omega_c*t_AD (photon absorption) and T_10 = -hbar*omega_c*t_AD (photon emission), while T_00=T_11=H_AD recover the bare diabatic coupling when the Franck-Condon dipole-self-energy parameter g_AD=0.
facts.source: Sharma & Chen 2024, text following Eq. (23) (arXiv:2406.17101)
facts.facet: Holstein-Tavis-Cummings model and cavity-modified electron transfer / Marcus theory (cross-check of Sharma & Chen 2024)

facts.claim: The collective Rabi splitting scales as 2*sqrt((N-1)|T_01|^2), i.e. sqrt(N-1) Tavis-Cummings/Dicke enhancement, with polariton energies Omega_pm^(I) = Delta/2 ± sqrt((Delta/2)^2+(N-1)|T_01|^2) and detuning Delta = hbar*omega_c - E_AD.
facts.source: Sharma & Chen 2024, Eq. (26) (arXiv:2406.17101)
facts.facet: Holstein-Tavis-Cummings model and cavity-modified electron transfer / Marcus theory (cross-check of Sharma & Chen 2024)

facts.claim: ET rates are computed with the classical Marcus rate K(V,E_a)=sqrt(pi/(E_r k_B T)) (|V|^2/(2 hbar)) exp(-E_a^2/(4 k_B T E_r)), activation energy E_a=E_fi+E_r, and effective reorganization energy E_r=lambda_v^2/(2 omega_v^2). This is algebraically identical to k_ET=(2pi/hbar)|V|^2 (1/sqrt(4 pi lambda k_B T)) exp(-(lambda+DeltaG)^2/(4 lambda k_B T)) with lambda=E_r and DeltaG=E_fi.
facts.source: Sharma & Chen 2024, Eq. (35) and text (arXiv:2406.17101)
facts.facet: Holstein-Tavis-Cummings model and cavity-modified electron transfer / Marcus theory (cross-check of Sharma & Chen 2024)

facts.claim: A non-monotonic dependence of the ET rate on N exists: the photon-coupled Path (c) rate has a maximum where the polariton PES becomes barrier-less (Omega_+ - hbar*omega_c = 0), giving turnover at N_max^(I)=1+hbar*omega_c/|T_01|^2 and N_max^(II)=1+hbar*omega_c/|T_10|^2 (Eq. 44).
facts.source: Sharma & Chen 2024, Eqs. (44a),(44b) and surrounding text (arXiv:2406.17101)
facts.facet: Holstein-Tavis-Cummings model and cavity-modified electron transfer / Marcus theory (cross-check of Sharma & Chen 2024)

facts.claim: CROSS-CHECK / CORRECTION to the task hypothesis: the published N_max is proportional to hbar*omega_c/|T_01|^2, NOT hbar*omega_c*E_AD/|T_01|^2. The donor-acceptor gap E_AD enters only through the resonance condition: the cavity is tuned to the gap (hbar*omega_c = E_AD = |E_A-E_D|), so at resonance the hbar*omega_c appearing in N_max equals E_AD. The turnover therefore does scale with the gap-at-resonance over the squared photon-coupling, but the literal extra factor of E_AD in the task's note is not present.
facts.source: Sharma & Chen 2024, Eqs. (26),(44) and resonance condition hbar*omega_c=1.0 eV=|E_AD| in Sec. IV (arXiv:2406.17101)
facts.facet: Holstein-Tavis-Cummings model and cavity-modified electron transfer / Marcus theory (cross-check of Sharma & Chen 2024)

facts.claim: For large N both the ET rates (Paths b and c) and polariton relaxation are suppressed: ET rate asymptotically scales as ~ e^{-N}, and the PR rate scales as ~ N^{-2}. The cavity-induced quantum yield rises linearly with N for small N (Dicke-like) then turns over and is suppressed for large N (the 'large-N problem' of polariton chemistry).
facts.source: Sharma & Chen 2024, text after Eq. (44) and abstract (arXiv:2406.17101)
facts.facet: Holstein-Tavis-Cummings model and cavity-modified electron transfer / Marcus theory (cross-check of Sharma & Chen 2024)

facts.claim: Numerical example: with |E_AD|=1.0 eV, H_AD=245 cm^-1 (~0.03 eV), E_r=1.0 eV, hbar*omega_v=80.6 cm^-1, k_B T=0.025 eV, hbar*omega_c=1.0 eV (resonant), hbar*omega_c|t_AD|=69 cm^-1 (|t_AD|=8.5e-3), |g_AD|=0.5, the two demonstrated turnover values are N_max=1636 and N_max=10785.
facts.source: Sharma & Chen 2024, Sec. IV (parameters) and Fig. 2 caption (arXiv:2406.17101)
facts.facet: Holstein-Tavis-Cummings model and cavity-modified electron transfer / Marcus theory (cross-check of Sharma & Chen 2024)

facts.claim: The light-matter interaction follows Semenov & Nitzan's confined-field ET treatment (PZW gauge, long-wavelength), which for a single molecule shows strong coupling to a cavity mode can enhance the nonadiabatic ET rate and eliminate the Marcus inverted region (make the path barrier-less). Sharma & Chen extend this to N molecules.
facts.source: A. Semenov & A. Nitzan, 'Electron transfer in confined electromagnetic fields,' J. Chem. Phys. 150, 174122 (2019), https://doi.org/10.1063/1.5095940; cited as Ref. 36 in Sharma & Chen 2024
facts.facet: Holstein-Tavis-Cummings model and cavity-modified electron transfer / Marcus theory (cross-check of Sharma & Chen 2024)

facts.claim: The collective-coupling PMET picture (rate enhancement from many molecules collectively coupled at small per-molecule coupling, with competition from dark states and cavity loss, yielding non-monotonic N dependence) is corroborated by Koessler, Mandal, Musser, Krauss & Huo, 'Polariton mediated electron transfer under the collective molecule-cavity coupling regime,' Chem. Sci. 16, 11644-11658 (2025), and the foundational Mandal, Krauss & Huo, J. Phys. Chem. B 124, 6321 (2020).
facts.source: Chem. Sci. 2025, DOI 10.1039/D5SC01911G (https://pubs.rsc.org/en/content/articlelanding/2025/sc/d5sc01911g); J. Phys. Chem. B 2020, DOI 10.1021/acs.jpcb.0c03227
facts.facet: Holstein-Tavis-Cummings model and cavity-modified electron transfer / Marcus theory (cross-check of Sharma & Chen 2024)

facts.claim: A follow-up unified Fermi-golden-rule rate theory by Chen and co-workers (extension to lossy cavities, reproducing Marcus and Marcus-Jortner limits) appears as arXiv:2511.04017 / J. Chem. Phys. 164, 024113 (2026).
facts.source: arXiv:2511.04017 (https://arxiv.org/pdf/2511.04017); J. Chem. Phys. 164, 024113 listing (https://pubs.aip.org/aip/jcp/article-abstract/164/2/024113)
facts.facet: Holstein-Tavis-Cummings model and cavity-modified electron transfer / Marcus theory (cross-check of Sharma & Chen 2024)

facts.claim: FOUNDATIONAL CLAIM: Thomas et al. (2016) reported that vibrational strong coupling to the cavity vacuum field SUPPRESSED the rate of ground-state deprotection of an alkynylsilane (1-phenyl-2-trimethylsilylacetylene) by a factor of up to 5.5 when the Si-C stretch was coupled, with significant changes in activation enthalpy and entropy interpreted as a switch from an associative to a dissociative transition state.
facts.source: Thomas et al., 'Ground-State Chemical Reactivity under Vibrational Coupling to the Vacuum Electromagnetic Field,' Angew. Chem. Int. Ed. 55, 11462 (2016), doi:10.1002/anie.201605504 (PMID 27529831)
facts.facet: The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

facts.claim: FOUNDATIONAL CLAIM (resonance + selectivity): Thomas/Ebbesen et al. (Science 2019) reported that VSC can 'tilt' a reactivity landscape, changing the branching between two silyl bond-cleavage sites (Si-C vs Si-O) and substantially altering activation entropy; the effect appeared in the dark (no pumping) and depended on which vibrational mode was coupled.
facts.source: Thomas et al., 'Tilting a ground-state reactivity landscape by vibrational strong coupling,' Science 363, 615 (2019), doi:10.1126/science.aau7742
facts.facet: The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

facts.claim: FOUNDATIONAL CLAIM (enzymes): Vergauwe et al. (2019) reported a ~4.5-fold DECREASE in pepsin's apparent second-order rate constant kcat/Km when the O-H stretch of water was strongly coupled, while coupling the water bending mode produced no measurable effect.
facts.source: Vergauwe et al., 'Modification of Enzyme Activity by Vibrational Strong Coupling of Water,' Angew. Chem. Int. Ed. 58, 15324 (2019), doi:10.1002/anie.201908876 (PMID 31449707)
facts.facet: The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

facts.claim: The reported effects are striking specifically because they are on the electronic GROUND state under thermal equilibrium with NO external optical pumping, and because they show a sharp RESONANCE: the rate change is significant only when a cavity mode is tuned into resonance with a vibrational mode, and tracks the Rabi splitting.
facts.source: Synthesis of Thomas et al. Science 363, 615 (2019), doi:10.1126/science.aau7742; and field reviews noting resonance behavior is hard to reproduce theoretically
facts.facet: The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

facts.claim: THE LARGE-N PROBLEM: VSC in these experiments is COLLECTIVE — one cavity mode couples to N~10^6-10^12 molecules. The measured Rabi splitting scales as Omega_R ∝ g*sqrt(N) (large, observable) but the coupling felt by any single molecule scales as g ∝ 1/sqrt(N) (negligible). Since a chemical reaction is a single-molecule event, it is theoretically very hard to explain how a per-molecule perturbation that vanishes in the thermodynamic limit can modify a thermally-activated barrier.
facts.source: Lindoy, Mandal & Reichman, 'Investigating the collective nature of cavity-modified chemical kinetics under vibrational strong coupling,' Nanophotonics 13, 2617 (2024), doi:10.1515/nanoph-2024-0026, arXiv:2403.03951
facts.facet: The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

facts.claim: Lindoy-Mandal-Reichman find that under EQUILIBRIUM (correlated) initial conditions the cavity rate modification VANISHES in the mean-field N→∞ limit; substantial modification appears only in the few-molecule limit. Under NON-EQUILIBRIUM (uncorrelated) initial conditions a collective resonant effect can persist to large N, but its magnitude is 'exceedingly small' — insufficient to explain the experimental observations, which they say point to features not in current models.
facts.source: Lindoy, Mandal & Reichman, Nanophotonics 13, 2617 (2024), doi:10.1515/nanoph-2024-0026, arXiv:2403.03951
facts.facet: The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

facts.claim: DARK-STATE ARGUMENT: Collective VSC of N molecules produces only 2 polaritons and N-1 'dark' modes parked at the bare vibrational frequency; ascribing thermal-reactivity changes only to the 2 polaritons is 'entropically unlikely.' Du & Yuen-Zhou (PRL 2022) argue that energetic DISORDER makes the dark modes semi-localized over ~2-3 molecules, opening enhanced vibrational-cooling channels that can catalyze a reaction; they compute a ~50% increase in a model electron-transfer rate, valid when dark-mode homogeneous linewidths are smaller than their energy spacings.
facts.source: Du & Yuen-Zhou, 'Catalysis by Dark States in Vibropolaritonic Chemistry,' Phys. Rev. Lett. 128, 096001 (2022), doi:10.1103/PhysRevLett.128.096001, arXiv:2104.07214
facts.facet: The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

facts.claim: Standard transition-state theory and equilibrium statistical mechanics predict essentially NO ground-state rate change from collective VSC: equilibrium populations/partition functions are unchanged when molecules are placed in the cavity, regardless of cavity frequency. Resonant rate modifications, where found theoretically, arise from NON-EQUILIBRIUM dynamical mechanisms rather than equilibrium property changes.
facts.source: Ke, 'Non-equilibrium origin of cavity-induced resonant modifications of chemical reactivities,' (2025), arXiv:2503.12568
facts.facet: The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

facts.claim: FAILED REPRODUCTION: Wiesehan & Xiong (2021) attempted to reproduce reported VSC catalysis of p-nitrophenyl acetate ester hydrolysis. Matching the same light-matter coupling strength and detuning, they did NOT observe the reported ~10-fold rate increase and saw no obvious detuning dependence, concluding cavity catalysis is sensitive to experimental details beyond the mere onset of VSC.
facts.source: Wiesehan & Xiong, 'Negligible rate enhancement from reported cooperative vibrational strong coupling catalysis,' J. Chem. Phys. 155, 241103 (2021), doi:10.1063/5.0077549 (PMID 34972384)
facts.facet: The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

facts.claim: FAILED/PARTIAL REPRODUCTION: Imperatore, Asbury & Giebink (2021) reported on the reproducibility of cavity-enhanced reaction rates in the VSC regime, finding that previously reported enhancements were difficult to reproduce and that subtle experimental factors strongly affect outcomes.
facts.source: Imperatore, Asbury & Giebink, 'Reproducibility of cavity-enhanced chemical reaction rates in the vibrational strong coupling regime,' J. Chem. Phys. 154, 191103 (2021), doi:10.1063/5.0046307
facts.facet: The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

facts.claim: FAILED REPRODUCTION: A piezo-tunable microcavity study aimed at reproducing Hiura et al.'s reported enhancement of cyanate-ion hydrolysis (via collective O-H stretch coupling of water) reproduced the reported vacuum Rabi splitting but observed NO change in reaction rate as the cavity was tuned in and out of the strong-coupling regime.
facts.source: Reported in the VSC reproducibility literature; see escholarship 'Reproducibility of Effects of Vibrational Strong Coupling on Chemical Reaction Rates' (UC eScholarship qt9pc1m1wx) and field reviews
facts.facet: The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

facts.claim: NULL RESULT (ultrafast): Transient-absorption study of CN radicals reacting with cyclohexane/chloroform under VSC of a C-H stretch found reaction rates UNCHANGED across extracavity, on-resonance, and off-resonance conditions, independent of coupling strength.
facts.source: 'Exploring the impact of vibrational cavity coupling strength on ultrafast CN + c-C6H12 reaction dynamics,' arXiv:2310.19133; PMC11635944
facts.facet: The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

facts.claim: METHODOLOGICAL CONFOUND (now considered an important caveat): Michon & Simpkins (JACS 2024) showed that submicron cavity-length NON-UNIFORMITY produces a UV-vis measurement artifact distorting apparent absorption peak position/amplitude and hence extracted reaction-rate constants; they introduced an effective molar absorption coefficient correction and demonstrated 2D spatial inhomogeneity of apparent rate constants. This implies some prior kinetic extractions may be artifacts of cavity inhomogeneity.
facts.source: Michon & Simpkins, 'Impact of Cavity Length Non-uniformity on Reaction Rate Extraction in Strong Coupling Experiments,' J. Am. Chem. Soc. 146, 30596 (2024), doi:10.1021/jacs.4c12269
facts.facet: The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

facts.claim: NOT UNIFORMLY NEGATIVE: There are independent and later positive reports with resonance dependence, e.g. a 2024 Nature Chemistry study reporting that vibrational weak AND strong coupling modify a chemical reaction via a cavity-mediated radiative-energy-transfer mechanism, indicating the experimental picture is mixed rather than fully debunked.
facts.source: 'Vibrational weak and strong coupling modify a chemical reaction via cavity-mediated radiative energy transfer,' Nat. Chem. (2024), doi:10.1038/s41557-024-01723-6 (PMID 39820806)
facts.facet: The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

facts.claim: PERSPECTIVE / FIELD SELF-ASSESSMENT: Simpkins, Dunkelberger & Owrutsky frame VSC chemistry as promising but beset by a lack of mechanistic understanding, potentially misleading spectroscopic interpretations, and a shortage of corroborating evidence; key open questions include WHICH reactions can be modified and to what extent.
facts.source: Simpkins, Dunkelberger & Owrutsky, 'Mode-Specific Chemistry through Vibrational Strong Coupling (or A Wish Come True),' J. Phys. Chem. C 125, 19081 (2021), doi:10.1021/acs.jpcc.1c05362
facts.facet: The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

facts.claim: FIELD CONSENSUS ON STATUS: The Chemical Reviews 2023 thematic issue on polaritonic chemistry and accompanying reviews acknowledge openly that reports of altered chemistry under VSC have met substantial skepticism due to irreproducible results and the lack of an accepted theoretical framework, and that the resonance behavior in particular has been hard to reproduce theoretically.
facts.source: Introduction: Polaritonic Chemistry, Chem. Rev. 123, 12037 (2023), doi:10.1021/acs.chemrev.3c00637; and 'Molecular Chemistry in Cavity Strong Coupling,' Chem. Rev. (2023), doi:10.1021/acs.chemrev.2c00748
facts.facet: The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

facts.claim: EARLY THEORY ATTEMPT: Galego, Climent, Garcia-Vidal & Feist (PRX 2019) proposed cavity Casimir-Polder forces could modify single-molecule ground-state reactivity, with the key parameter being the permanent dipole and its change along the reaction coordinate. However, this single-molecule/ultrastrong-coupling mechanism does not straightforwardly carry over to the collective, weakly-per-molecule-coupled regime of the experiments — illustrating the theory-experiment gap.
facts.source: Galego, Climent, Garcia-Vidal & Feist, 'Cavity Casimir-Polder Forces and Their Effects in Ground-State Chemical Reactivity,' Phys. Rev. X 9, 021057 (2019), doi:10.1103/PhysRevX.9.021057, arXiv:1807.10846
facts.facet: The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

facts.claim: SETTLED POINTS: (i) The Rabi splitting / formation of vibrational polaritons is real and routinely measured in IR; (ii) equilibrium thermodynamic quantities are unchanged by the cavity; (iii) per-single-molecule coupling is negligible; (iv) cavity-uniformity and spectroscopic-extraction artifacts are real and must be controlled. UNSETTLED: whether thermal ground-state rates are genuinely modified in most systems, the magnitude/reproducibility, the microscopic mechanism, and the origin of the sharp resonance and how any effect survives 1/sqrt(N) collective dilution.
facts.source: Synthesis of Lindoy et al. Nanophotonics 13, 2617 (2024); Du & Yuen-Zhou PRL 128, 096001 (2022); Michon & Simpkins JACS 146, 30596 (2024); Simpkins et al. JPCC 125, 19081 (2021)
facts.facet: The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.

facts.claim: The canonical polariton dispersion plot (Houdre et al. Fig. 2, from Phys. Rev. Lett. 73 (1994) 2043) puts Energy (eV) on the vertical axis (range ~1.31-1.36 eV), in-plane Wave vector (m^-1) on the bottom axis (0 to ~4x10^6 m^-1), and a TWIN top axis 'External emission angle (deg)' from -10 to +40; the LP and UP data (red points) avoid-cross the dashed bare-exciton (flat) and bare-cavity (parabolic) lines, with the Rabi splitting Omega = 7.1 meV marked by a vertical double-headed arrow at the closest approach.
facts.source: Houdre, Stanley, Oesterle, Weisbuch, 'Strong coupling regime in semiconductor microcavities', C. R. Physique 3 (2002) 15-27, Fig. 2
facts.facet: Visualization conventions in the polariton / cavity-QED literature (axes, units, color maps, typical ranges) with cited canonical figures, for 8 plot types used in molecular-polariton and microcavity-polariton simulations.

facts.claim: The vacuum Rabi splitting appears in absorption/reflectivity as a doublet of two peaks separated by Omega; for high-quality InGaAs GaAs QW microcavities Omega is ~7-9 meV (e.g. Omega=8.8 meV in absorption at T=100 K), linewidths gamma a few meV, plotted as Absorption vs Energy (eV) ~1.33-1.35 eV.
facts.source: Houdre et al., C. R. Physique 3 (2002), Fig. 1 (from Phys. Rev. B 49 (1994) 16761)
facts.facet: Visualization conventions in the polariton / cavity-QED literature (axes, units, color maps, typical ranges) with cited canonical figures, for 8 plot types used in molecular-polariton and microcavity-polariton simulations.

facts.claim: Rabi splitting scales as Omega ∝ sqrt(f_osc N_QW / L_cavity) for inorganic QWs and as Omega_R = 2g sqrt(N) for molecular ensembles; this sqrt(N) collective enhancement is the standard reason ensembles (N~10^6-10^12 per mode) reach strong coupling and is the key axis-scaling behind rate-vs-collective-coupling plots.
facts.source: Houdre et al. C.R. Physique 3 (2002); Bhatt et al. 'Molecular Polaritons...' (PMC10941193); Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo et al. Nat. Commun. 10 (2019) 4685
facts.facet: Visualization conventions in the polariton / cavity-QED literature (axes, units, color maps, typical ranges) with cited canonical figures, for 8 plot types used in molecular-polariton and microcavity-polariton simulations.

facts.claim: Anticrossing vs detuning is conventionally shown by plotting the two measured polariton peak energies as a function of cavity-exciton detuning (swept from negative through zero to positive); the branches form two hyperbola-like curves whose minimum separation at zero detuning equals the Rabi splitting, with the bare cavity and bare exciton drawn as crossing straight lines. The Houdre review explicitly states the eigenstate energies 'vs. detuning delta (delta = E_phot - E_at) show[] an anticrossing behavior near resonance.'
facts.source: Houdre et al., C. R. Physique 3 (2002) 15-27, Section 1; ResearchGate 'Bragg polariton branches vs detuning showing characteristic anticrossing'
facts.facet: Visualization conventions in the polariton / cavity-QED literature (axes, units, color maps, typical ranges) with cited canonical figures, for 8 plot types used in molecular-polariton and microcavity-polariton simulations.

facts.claim: Angle-resolved photoluminescence is plotted as a 2D intensity map I(E, theta): emission energy (eV) on one axis and emission angle theta (deg) on the other; PL intensity is shown with a hot/sequential color map; the fitted LP, UP, bare-cavity, and bare-exciton dispersion curves are overlaid as lines. The angle axis maps one-to-one to in-plane momentum because each emission angle corresponds to one k_parallel.
facts.source: Web search of microcavity ARPL literature (ResearchGate angle-resolved PL spectra figures); Houdre et al. C.R. Physique 3 (2002) (twin angle/k axes)
facts.facet: Visualization conventions in the polariton / cavity-QED literature (axes, units, color maps, typical ranges) with cited canonical figures, for 8 plot types used in molecular-polariton and microcavity-polariton simulations.

facts.claim: Hopfield coefficients are plotted as |C_phot|^2 and |C_exc|^2 (each between 0 and 1, summing to unity) versus detuning or versus k; the LP photon fraction decreases from 1 to 0 as detuning goes from negative to positive and the two fractions cross at 0.5 at resonance (where the mixing coefficients C_ij = 1/sqrt(2)).
facts.source: Houdre et al. C. R. Physique 3 (2002) (C_ij = 1/sqrt(2) at resonance, |C_ij|^2 weighting); Bhatt et al. review (PMC10941193)
facts.facet: Visualization conventions in the polariton / cavity-QED literature (axes, units, color maps, typical ranges) with cited canonical figures, for 8 plot types used in molecular-polariton and microcavity-polariton simulations.

facts.claim: The standard Bloch sphere convention places the ground state |0> at the north pole and the excited state |1> at the south pole, with |psi>=cos(theta/2)|0>+e^{i phi}sin(theta/2)|1>, theta in [0,pi] the polar angle and phi in [0,2pi) the azimuthal angle; Cartesian axes are <sigma_x>, <sigma_y>, <sigma_z>; surface points are pure states and interior points (rho=(I+r.sigma)/2, |r|<=1) are mixed states.
facts.source: 'Bloch sphere', Wikipedia (en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bloch_sphere); Nielsen & Chuang convention
facts.facet: Visualization conventions in the polariton / cavity-QED literature (axes, units, color maps, typical ranges) with cited canonical figures, for 8 plot types used in molecular-polariton and microcavity-polariton simulations.

facts.claim: Marcus diagrams plot free energy G (vertical, energy units) vs reaction coordinate q (horizontal); two intersecting parabolas of equal curvature represent reactant and product diabatic states, offset vertically by the driving force Delta G-degrees and horizontally by their displaced minima; the reorganization energy lambda is the vertical gap at the reactant geometry and the activation barrier Delta G-double-dagger = (Delta G-deg + lambda)^2 / (4 lambda) sits at the crossing. Inverted regime occurs when -Delta G-deg > lambda.
facts.source: Tokmakoff (MIT), 'Marcus Theory for Electron Transfer', Chemistry LibreTexts 19.4
facts.facet: Visualization conventions in the polariton / cavity-QED literature (axes, units, color maps, typical ranges) with cited canonical figures, for 8 plot types used in molecular-polariton and microcavity-polariton simulations.

facts.claim: To show how a cavity reshapes the barrier, the canonical figure (Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo et al. Fig. 3) plots potential energy E vs the slow coordinate q_S with the reactant parabola, the product ground-state parabola (in the Marcus inverted regime, drawn orange), and Rabi-split product manifolds: lower polariton (red), upper polariton (purple) separated by hΩ, plus the dense set of dark states (green); labeled energies are the barrier E‡_000, the driving force ΔE, the Rabi splitting hΩ, the vibrational quantum hω_P, the reorganization energy λ_S, and the displacement d_S.
facts.source: Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo, Ribeiro, Yuen-Zhou, 'Resonant catalysis of thermally-activated chemical reactions with vibrational polaritons', Nat. Commun. 10 (2019) 4685, Fig. 3
facts.facet: Visualization conventions in the polariton / cavity-QED literature (axes, units, color maps, typical ranges) with cited canonical figures, for 8 plot types used in molecular-polariton and microcavity-polariton simulations.

facts.claim: Rate vs collective coupling / N is conventionally plotted as the ratio of in-cavity to out-of-cavity rate coefficient k_in/k_out versus the collective coupling g*sqrt(N)/omega_P at several detunings; the curves are bell-shaped (turnover): the rate rises as the lower-polariton channel activation energy falls, then turns over and drops once the LP channel enters the Marcus inverted regime, with the maximum largest near resonance. (Calculations in the source span 1 <= N <= 10^11.)
facts.source: Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo et al., Nat. Commun. 10 (2019) 4685, Fig. 4
facts.facet: Visualization conventions in the polariton / cavity-QED literature (axes, units, color maps, typical ranges) with cited canonical figures, for 8 plot types used in molecular-polariton and microcavity-polariton simulations.

facts.claim: For vibrational-strong-coupling rate-vs-detuning, the convention is to plot the rate modification k/k0 (dimensionless ratio) versus cavity frequency omega_c (in cm^-1) showing a SHARP resonance peak centered exactly at the molecular vibrational frequency (e.g. resonance at omega_c = omega_0 = 1190 cm^-1), reproducing the experimental observation that effects are maximal at light-matter resonance.
facts.source: Ying, Mandal, Huo et al., 'Resonance theory of vibrational polariton chemistry at the normal incidence', PNAS / PMC11636501, Fig. 5(c)
facts.facet: Visualization conventions in the polariton / cavity-QED literature (axes, units, color maps, typical ranges) with cited canonical figures, for 8 plot types used in molecular-polariton and microcavity-polariton simulations.

facts.claim: In the collective-coupling energy-level (Jablonski-style) diagram, N molecules + 1 cavity mode produce two bright polariton states (lower polariton LP and upper polariton UP) split by Omega_R = 2g*sqrt(N), plus a manifold of (N-1) DARK states that lie at the bare molecular transition energy between LP and UP; for large N this 'dark-state reservoir' vastly outnumbers the two bright states and is drawn as a dense band of levels.
facts.source: Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo et al. Nat. Commun. 10 (2019) 4685 (two polaritons + N-1 dark states, Eqs. 3-4); Mandal, Huo et al. Chem. Rev. 123 (2023) 9786; Bhatt et al. review PMC10941193
facts.facet: Visualization conventions in the polariton / cavity-QED literature (axes, units, color maps, typical ranges) with cited canonical figures, for 8 plot types used in molecular-polariton and microcavity-polariton simulations.

facts.claim: In MLJ vibropolariton theory the collective polariton frequencies are omega_±(N) = (omega_0+omega_P)/2 ± Omega_N/2 with Omega_N = sqrt(4 g^2 N + Delta^2), the dark states omega_D = omega_P, and the single-molecule coupling g = -mu sqrt(hbar omega/(2 V epsilon_0)); these define the energies placed on the level diagram and the activation energies of each reactive channel.
facts.source: Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo, Ribeiro, Yuen-Zhou, Nat. Commun. 10 (2019) 4685, Eqs. 3-4 and surrounding text
facts.facet: Visualization conventions in the polariton / cavity-QED literature (axes, units, color maps, typical ranges) with cited canonical figures, for 8 plot types used in molecular-polariton and microcavity-polariton simulations.

facts.claim: Color/line conventions in the dispersion literature: lower polariton drawn in red/lower color and upper polariton in blue/purple; bare exciton and bare cavity drawn as dashed/dotted guide lines (flat exciton, parabolic cavity); 2D PL/reflectivity maps use a perceptually-uniform or hot color map for intensity rather than rainbow. (General convention inferred across the cited figures.)
facts.source: Houdre et al. C. R. Physique 3 (2002), Figs. 2,5; Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo Nat. Commun. Fig. 3
facts.facet: Visualization conventions in the polariton / cavity-QED literature (axes, units, color maps, typical ranges) with cited canonical figures, for 8 plot types used in molecular-polariton and microcavity-polariton simulations.

paper.title: Unraveling abnormal collective effects via the non-monotonic number dependence of electron transfer in confined electromagnetic fields
paper.authors: Shravan Kumar Sharma and Hsing-Ta Chen (Department of Chemistry and Biochemistry, University of Notre Dame, Notre Dame, Indiana 46556, USA)
paper.venue: The Journal of Chemical Physics 161, 104102 (2024); doi: 10.1063/5.0225434; JCP Special Topic on Polaritonics for Next Generation Materials

pdfFindings.summary: The paper formulates a Holstein-Tavis-Cummings (HTC) model for N identical donor-acceptor (D-A) pairs collectively coupled to a single confined cavity photon mode, and treats the non-adiabatic electron transfer (ET) of a single 'active' reacting molecule (j=1) using a spin-boson description with Marcus theory, while the other N-1 'non-reacting' molecules act as a thermal/fluctuation bath. After a polaron (Lang-Firsov-type) unitary transformation, the dipole self-energy is absorbed into an effective, photon-dressed diabatic coupling, and the dressed-state Hamiltonian is diagonalized under a rotating-wave approximation (RWA) into upper/lower polariton states P_+/P_- plus N-2 dark states. Two RWA regimes are analyzed: RWA-(I) absorption (E_AD>0, |D}1> resonant with |{A_j}0>) and RWA-(II) emission (E_AD<0, |D}0> resonant with |{A_j}1>). Cavity-modified Marcus ET rates from the polariton states (Eqs. 35-37) and Fermi-golden-rule polariton relaxation (PR) rates to the dark states (Eqs. 38-41) are derived analytically. The central result is a NON-MONOTONIC dependence of the photon-coupled ET rate on N: the effective Rabi splitting scales as sqrt((N-1)|T01|^2), pushing the polariton potential energy surfaces toward the barrier-less condition; the ET rate of the photon-coupled path increases (peaks at a turnover N_max where the activation energy vanishes) and then is exponentially suppressed for large N, while the PR rate scales as ~N^{-2}. The turnover number is N_max = 1 + hbar*omega_c/|T01| (absorption) or 1 + hbar*omega_c/|T10| (emission). Cavity-induced quantum yields scale linearly with N at small N (Dicke-model agreement, QY_b up to ~1, QY_c < 0.5) and are suppressed at large N because polariton relaxation dominates. A slow-bath (small omega_v) regime makes cavity-enhanced ET more effective, and the contribution of path (c) is robust against thermal fluctuations because N_max depends only on the effective coupling T and omega_c, not on omega_v.

key_equations.name: Total HTC Hamiltonian

variables.meaning: cavity photon Hamiltonian, = hbar*omega_c * a^dag a (zero-point energy neglected)
molecular subsystem Hamiltonian of the jth donor-acceptor pair

key_equations.context: Ensemble of N non-interacting molecules coupled to a single quantized cavity mode; long-wavelength approximation.

key_equations.name: Molecular (spin-boson) Hamiltonian per pair
key_equations.latex: \hat{H}_{M,j} = E_D |D_j\rangle\langle D_j| + \left(E_A + \sum_\alpha c_{\alpha,j}(\hat{b}^\dagger_{j,\alpha} + \hat{b}_{j,\alpha})\right)|A_j\rangle\langle A_j| + H_{AD}|A_j\rangle\langle D_j| + H_{DA}|D_j\rangle\langle A_j| + \sum_\alpha \hbar\omega_\alpha\left(\hat{b}^\dagger_{j,\alpha}\hat{b}_{j,\alpha} + \tfrac{1}{2}\right)

variables.meaning: diabatic donor / acceptor electronic states of jth pair (excess electron on donor / acceptor)
electronic (diabatic) coupling between donor and acceptor; H_{AD} = <A_j|H_{M,j}|D_j>
linear coupling of acceptor electronic state to boson mode alpha

key_equations.context: Spin-boson model for each D-A pair; nuclear modes treated semiclassically as classical harmonic oscillators (primary mode + thermal bath). Identical pairs.

key_equations.name: Molecular Hamiltonian matrix (semiclassical, primary coordinate)

variables.meaning: classical position and momentum of the primary nuclear mode of pair j (effective mass m absorbed)
donor / acceptor diabatic potential energy surfaces (see Eq. 4)

key_equations.context: Primary mode R_j follows the Langevin equation; obtained after recasting bath into primary mode + secondary thermal bath.

key_equations.name: Diabatic potential energy surfaces
key_equations.latex: V_D(R_j) = E_D + \tfrac{1}{2}\omega_v^2 R_j^2, \qquad V_A(R_j) = E_A + \lambda_v R_j + \tfrac{1}{2}\omega_v^2 R_j^2

variables.meaning: characteristic frequency of the primary vibrational mode (j-independent)
variables.typical: 80.6 cm^-1 (baseline); varied over slow/fast bath in Fig.4

variables.meaning: primary-mode (system-bath) coupling strength; sets reorganization energy
variables.typical: lambda_v^2 = 2*omega_v^2 in Fig.4; E_r = 1.0 eV baseline

variables.meaning: donor-acceptor energy gap E_AD = E_A - E_D

key_equations.context: Harmonic diabatic PES with identical curvature; reorganization energy E_r = lambda_v^2/(2 omega_v^2).

key_equations.name: Langevin equation for primary mode

variables.typical: gamma = 0.065 s^-1 (as printed in paper; see uncertainty)

variables.meaning: Markovian Gaussian stochastic random force (white noise)

key_equations.where: p.3 (unnumbered Langevin eq.) and surrounding text
key_equations.context: Note: paper writes the friction term as -gamma_v * P-dot (transcribed as printed); standard Langevin would have -gamma_v * R-dot. Records the local thermal fluctuation of jth molecule.

key_equations.name: Light-matter interaction (dipole + self-energy)

variables.meaning: dipole operator of excess electron in jth pair
cavity electric field at molecule position

key_equations.context: Minimal-coupling QED in Power-Zienau-Woolley (PZW) gauge, long-wavelength approximation; H_DS is the dipole self-energy term.

key_equations.name: Cavity field at molecule

variables.meaning: cavity photon frequency (= c|k|); resonant with D-A gap

key_equations.context: Long-wavelength approx sets exp(ik.r) ~ 1 and r_j=0; all molecules see same field E(0) = i*xi*sqrt(omega_c/(2 V eps0))(a - a^dag).

key_equations.name: Electric dipole coupling Hamiltonian
key_equations.latex: \hat{H}_{DE} = \hbar\omega_c(\hat{a} - \hat{a}^\dagger)\sum_j\left[ g_D|D_j\rangle\langle D_j| + g_A|A_j\rangle\langle A_j| + t_{AD}|A_j\rangle\langle D_j| + t_{DA}|D_j\rangle\langle A_j| \right]

variables.meaning: dimensionless dipole couplings for donor / acceptor diagonal dipoles
dimensionless radiative coupling for the D-A transition dipole

key_equations.context: Idealized: N molecules equally coupled to cavity, no spatial/orientational disorder, no intermolecular dipole-dipole.

key_equations.name: Dimensionless coupling definitions
key_equations.context: Dipole moments j-independent (identical molecules).

key_equations.name: Dipole self-energy term
key_equations.latex: \hat{H}_{DS} = \hbar\omega_c\sum_j\left[|g_D|^2|D_j\rangle\langle D_j| + |g_A|^2|A_j\rangle\langle A_j| + |t_{AD}|^2\right] - \hbar\omega_c(g_D + g_A)\sum_j\left[t_{AD}|A_j\rangle\langle D_j| + t_{DA}|D_j\rangle\langle A_j|\right]
key_equations.context: Self-energy does NOT involve photon absorption/emission (local molecular term); second term gives an effective contribution to the diabatic coupling, affecting ET. Important in strong-coupling regime.

key_equations.name: Polaron transformation generator
key_equations.latex: \hat{\mathcal{S}} = \sum_j\left( g_D^*\hat{a}^\dagger - g_D\hat{a}\right)|D_j\rangle\langle D_j| + \left( g_A^*\hat{a}^\dagger - g_A\hat{a}\right)|A_j\rangle\langle A_j|, \qquad \hat{H} = e^{\hat{\mathcal{S}}}\hat{H}e^{-\hat{\mathcal{S}}}

variables.meaning: anti-Hermitian generator of polaron (Lang-Firsov-type) unitary transformation

key_equations.context: Eliminates the quadratic light-matter coupling; cavity Hamiltonian H_C unchanged.

key_equations.name: Photonic shift (Franck-Condon) operator

variables.meaning: Franck-Condon shift parameter, proportional to position difference of excess electron between A and D

key_equations.context: Dresses the diabatic coupling after polaron transformation; PES unchanged.

key_equations.name: Effective (polaron-transformed) Hamiltonian
key_equations.context: Standard coupled electron-nuclei form; PES V_D, V_A unchanged by polaron transformation.

key_equations.name: Effective diabatic coupling operator
key_equations.context: Effective coupling becomes a photonic operator; includes the diabatic coupling H_AD, a self-energy shift, and a photon absorption/emission term.

key_equations.name: Effective diabatic coupling matrix element (dressed states)

variables.meaning: effective coupling between photon-number states l (donor manifold) and m (acceptor manifold)
matrix element of the shift operator (Eq.21)

key_equations.context: Key cavity-modified coupling. For g_AD=0: F_lm=delta_lm, T_00=T_11=H_AD (standard ET), T_01=hbar*omega_c*t_AD (absorption), T_10=-hbar*omega_c*t_AD (emission).

key_equations.name: Shift-operator matrix element (Laguerre)

variables.meaning: associated Laguerre polynomial; L_0^k=1, L_1^k=-x+k+1, L_2^k=(1/2)[x^2-2(k+2)x+(k+1)(k+2)]

key_equations.context: F_lm = 0 if l or m negative. Valid for l,m >= 0.

key_equations.name: Polaron-transformed effective Hamiltonian in dressed basis

variables.meaning: all electrons in donor states, m cavity photons
excess electron of jth molecule in acceptor, others in donor, m photons

key_equations.context: Three ET channels: l=m (ET without photon), l<m (photon absorption-mediated ET), l>m (ET-induced light emission).

key_equations.name: Dressed-state diagonal PES
key_equations.latex: V_{\{D\}m}(\{R\}) = \sum_{j=1}^{N} V_D(R_j) + m\hbar\omega_c, \qquad V_{\{A_1\}m}(\{R\}) = V_A(R_1) + \sum_{j'\neq 1} V_D(R_{j'}) + m\hbar\omega_c

variables.meaning: photon number (restricted to m=0,1 in this work)

key_equations.name: Polariton energies RWA-(I)

key_equations.name: Polariton mixing angle Theta(T)

key_equations.name: Polariton energies RWA-(II) emission

key_equations.context: RWA-(II): E_D>E_A, |{D}0> and |{A_j}1> resonant, coupled via T_10. Polariton states Eqs.(33),(34).

key_equations.name: Upper/lower polariton states RWA-(II)
key_equations.context: Emission case; D->A transition coupled to a cavity photon emission.

key_equations.name: Marcus ET rate (cavity-modified, general)

activation energy; E_fi = energy difference between final and initial states

key_equations.context: Standard nonadiabatic Marcus rate; E_a depends on the polariton energy Omega so it becomes barrier-less at the turnover N_max.

key_equations.name: ET rates from polariton states RWA-(I) absorption
key_equations.context: Path (c): P_+/P_- -> {A_1}0 (Eqs.36a,b) shows turnover (max when Omega_+^(1)=0). Standard path (a): k_{{D}0->{A_1}0}=K(T_00,E_AD) recovers single-pair ET.

key_equations.name: ET rates from polariton states RWA-(II) emission
key_equations.context: Path (c): P_-/P_+ -> {A_1}0 (Eqs.37c,d) max when Omega_-^(II)+hbar*omega_c=0. Standard path recovers single-pair ET k_{{D}1->{A_1}1}=K(T_11,E_DA).

key_equations.name: Polariton relaxation (PR) rate, Fermi golden rule
key_equations.context: Note: paper writes sum k=1..N-1 but dark states are k=1..N-2 (see uncertainty). PR is relaxation of polariton to dark states via local molecular fluctuations, not cavity loss.

key_equations.name: Brownian spectral density (PR)
key_equations.where: p.8 Eq.(39); also Appendix B Eq.(B8) writes J(Omega)=2 gamma kB T/((Omega^2-omega_v^2)^2 + gamma^2 Omega^2)
key_equations.context: Spectral density of fluctuating modes R_k(t) following Langevin (Brownian) dynamics; fast-modulation limit.

key_equations.name: PR rates absorption RWA-(I)
key_equations.where: p.8 Eqs.(40a),(40b); Appendix B Eqs.(B9),(B10) give the (N-1)*lambda_v^2/(N*hbar) form reducing to lambda_v^2/hbar
key_equations.context: PR rate at the polariton energy. At resonance theta=pi/4. Asymptotic scaling Gamma ~ N^{-2} for large N.

key_equations.name: PR rates emission RWA-(II)
key_equations.context: Emission-case PR. At resonance Gamma_+^(I)=Gamma_-^(I) and Gamma_+^(II)=Gamma_-^(II); PR ~ N^{-2} for large N.

key_equations.name: Resonance Rabi splitting (Delta=0)
key_equations.context: At resonance Delta=0, cos theta = sin theta = 1/sqrt(2), independent of N. Rabi splitting increases as sqrt(N-1).

key_equations.name: N_max turnover (barrier-less condition) — CENTRAL RESULT

variables.meaning: molecule number at which the photon-coupled ET path becomes barrier-less (maximum ET rate); turnover point
variables.typical: for hbar*omega_c=1.0 eV, |T_01|=69 cm^-1 ~ 8.55e-3 eV => N_max ~ 1 + 117 ~ 118 (order 1e2-1e3 as in Fig.2)

variables.meaning: magnitude of effective photon-coupled diabatic coupling (= hbar*omega_c*|t_AD| when g_AD=0)

key_equations.context: Derived by setting Omega_+^(1)-hbar*omega_c=0 (abs.) or Omega_-^(II)+hbar*omega_c=0 (emis.) at resonance so the ET path activation energy vanishes (barrier-less). IMPORTANT: the printed N_max formula depends ONLY on hbar*omega_c and |T_01|/|T_10|; E_AD enters implicitly through the resonance condition |E_AD|=hbar*omega_c used in deriving Omega_pm and is NOT an explicit variable in Eq.(44). For large N>>N_max both ET path (b),(c) decrease with N, asymptotic ET rate k^(I) propto k^(II) propto e^{-N}; PR rates scale as N^{-2}.

key_equations.name: Cavity-induced quantum yields RWA-(I)

variables.meaning: quantum yields of path (b) and path (c) (ratio of final acceptor population, from polaritons, to cavity photon occupation)

key_equations.context: QY excludes standard ET path (a); counts only cavity-modified acceptor population. QY_b ~ N (linear, Dicke) for small N; suppressed for large N. QY_c < 0.5 because only one polariton contributes.

Diabatic electronic coupling between donor and acceptor (= T_00 = T_11 when g_AD=0)
Reorganization energy = lambda_v^2/(2 omega_v^2)
parameters.range: fixed at 1.0 eV in Fig.4; equals |E_AD| for barrier-less at N=1

parameters.meaning: Characteristic frequency of the vibrational primary mode
parameters.range: varied ~1e-2 to ~1e0 (slow to fast bath) in Fig.4; relative units on omega_v axis

parameters.meaning: Damping (friction) coefficient of the primary mode in Langevin dynamics
parameters.default: 0.065 s^-1 (printed value; units suspicious — see uncertainties)

parameters.meaning: Thermal energy / bath temperature
Cavity photon energy, resonant with the D-A gap
Radiative coupling between electronic transition and cavity photon (= |T_01| = |T_10| when g_AD=0)
Dimensionless radiative (transition-dipole) coupling parameter
Franck-Condon shift-operator coupling parameter (electron position difference between A and D)
Primary-mode (system-bath) coupling strength
Cavity photon number (restricted to vacuum m=0 and single photon m=1)

key_results.claim: The cavity-modified ET rate has a NON-MONOTONIC dependence on N: it rises with N (collective enhancement) up to a turnover N_max where the path becomes barrier-less, then is exponentially suppressed for large N.
key_results.evidence: Effective Rabi splitting Omega_pm propto sqrt((N-1)|T_01|^2) (Eqs.26,42) pushes polariton PES toward barrier-less; Marcus rate (Eq.35) peaks when activation energy E_a=0 at N_max; for N>>N_max, k^(I) propto k^(II) propto e^{-N}. Fig.2 shows P_+ -> {A_1}1 (I) and P_- -> {A_1}0 (II) peaking near the dotted line N_max.

key_results.claim: The turnover number is N_max^(I) = 1 + hbar*omega_c/|T_01| (absorption) and N_max^(II) = 1 + hbar*omega_c/|T_10| (emission). It depends on omega_c and the effective coupling T but NOT on omega_v (independent of bath frequency).
key_results.evidence: Eqs.(44a),(44b) printed explicitly. Sec.IV D states 'N_max [Eq.(44)] is determined by the effective coupling T and the cavity photon frequency hbar*omega_c, yet independent of omega_v', explaining why path (c) QY is robust against thermal fluctuation.

key_results.claim: E_AD does not appear explicitly in the printed N_max formula; its role enters via the resonance/barrier-less condition |E_AD| = hbar*omega_c and the requirement E_r = |E_AD| (so N=1 is barrier-less reference). With |E_AD|=hbar*omega_c=1.0 eV and E_r=1.0 eV, the single-pair (N=1) activation energy is zero.
key_results.evidence: Sec.IV B: 'we choose |E_AD| = hbar*omega_c and the activation energy is zero for N=1.' Resonance Delta = hbar*omega_c - |E_AD| = 0 used throughout. N_max derived from Omega_+^(1)-hbar*omega_c=0 at resonance.

key_results.claim: The polariton relaxation (PR) rate to dark states decreases with N as ~N^{-2}, and dominates over ET for large N, causing overall suppression of cavity-enhanced ET and quantum yield (the large-N problem, but driven by Marcus turnover, not free-energy shift).
key_results.evidence: Sec.IV A: 'the asymptotic scaling is Gamma^(I) propto Gamma^(II) propto N^{-2}.' Fig.2 grey dashed PR lines decrease with N. PR (~N^{-2}) decays slower than ET (~e^{-N}), so PR dominates as N->infinity.

key_results.claim: Cavity-induced quantum yield scales LINEARLY with N for small N (QY_b propto N), agreeing with the Dicke model; QY_b can reach ~1; QY_c is bounded < 0.5.
key_results.evidence: Sec.IV C and Fig.3: 'QY_b propto N, which agrees with the linear dependence on N predicted by the Dicke model'; 'QY_b can be as large as QY_b ~ 1 since both P_+ contribute equally'; 'QY_c is limited to QY_c <= 0.5' because only one polariton contributes to that ET.

key_results.claim: In the slow-bath regime (small omega_v) cavity-enhanced ET is more effective; in the fast-bath regime the PR rate grows (Gamma propto omega_c^2 / ... ) and QY_b is suppressed, while QY_c (path c) is robust against thermal fluctuation.
key_results.evidence: Sec.IV D, Fig.4: QY heatmaps vs (omega_v, N). QY_b (red dashed contour) suppressed in fast-bath regime; QY_c (blue dotted contour) more robust because N_max is independent of omega_v.

key_results.claim: After polaron transformation the dipole self-energy is fully absorbed into the effective diabatic coupling T_lm via the shift-operator matrix elements F_lm (associated Laguerre polynomials); for g_AD=0 the standard ET (T_00=T_11=H_AD) is recovered and T_01=hbar*omega_c*t_AD, T_10=-hbar*omega_c*t_AD.
