uncertainties: I could not directly fetch several paywalled primary sources (Thomas 2016, Science 2019, Vergauwe 2019, the Chem Rev editorials, and the Michon-Simpkins JACS full text) due to 402/403 errors; the numbers quoted (5.5x suppression, 4.5x enzyme decrease, 10x ester-hydrolysis claim) come from publisher abstracts, PubMed, and well-aligned secondary search summaries rather than the full PDFs. They are consistent across multiple sources but should ideally be confirmed against the published figures.

The exact original magnitude of the cyanate/cyanate-ester hydrolysis enhancement reported by Hiura et al., and the precise authorship/venue of the piezo-tunable-cavity null-result paper, were inferred from search summaries and the UC eScholarship record; I did not open the full text, so attribution of that specific null result (likely a Giebink/Penn State or related group) is medium confidence.

Whether the field has reached genuine 'consensus' on any reproducible positive VSC effect is itself contested. Some groups (Ebbesen, and later Nature Chemistry 2024 work) maintain robust resonant effects exist; critics maintain most are artifacts or irreproducible. I have presented this as a live disagreement rather than declaring a winner.

The Du-Yuen-Zhou dark-state-catalysis mechanism and the Lindoy-Mandal-Reichman / Ke non-equilibrium mechanism are model results (~50% and 'exceedingly small' effects respectively); neither has been shown to quantitatively reproduce a specific experimental dataset including the sharp resonance and the correct collective scaling simultaneously. The statement that 'no theory yet reproduces all three' is my synthesis and could be outdated by very recent (2025-2026) work I did not retrieve.

Equation conventions for per-molecule coupling differ across papers (constant-density 'g/sqrt(N)' vs constant-volume formulations where individual coupling is N-independent and Rabi splitting grows as sqrt(N)). I transcribed the Lindoy et al. constant-density form (their Eq. 9) via a fast-model extraction of the HTML; the exact prefactor/notation should be verified against the published equation.

webFindings.facet: Visualization conventions in the polariton / cavity-QED literature (axes, units, color maps, typical ranges) with cited canonical figures, for 8 plot types used in molecular-polariton and microcavity-polariton simulations.
webFindings.summary: This report fixes the plotting conventions physicists expect for each of the 8 requested figure types, anchored to canonical sources. (1) Polariton dispersion is plotted as Energy (eV, or meV offset) on the vertical axis vs in-plane wavevector k_parallel (units m^-1 or um^-1, range ~0 to a few x10^6 m^-1) on the bottom axis, very commonly with a TWIN top axis of external emission angle in degrees (~-10 to +40 deg); the two solid branches (LP lower/red, UP upper/blue) avoid-cross the dashed bare exciton (flat horizontal) and bare cavity (parabolic, upward) lines, with the minimum gap = Rabi splitting hΩ (tens of meV for inorganic QWs, e.g. 7-9 meV in Houdre/Weisbuch GaAs; up to 100s of meV for organics). The canonical figure is Houdre et al. C.R. Physique 3 (2002) Fig. 2 (reproduced from Phys. Rev. Lett. 73 (1994) 2043). (2) Anticrossing vs detuning: plot the two measured peak energies vs cavity-exciton detuning Δ (meV, swept negative through 0 to positive), giving two hyperbola-like branches whose closest approach at Δ=0 equals hΩ; bare cavity and exciton appear as crossing straight lines. (3) Angle-resolved PL maps are 2D intensity images I(E, θ): energy (eV) on one axis, emission angle θ (deg) on the other, with a hot/perceptually-uniform color map for PL intensity and the fitted LP/UP/cavity/exciton curves overlaid as lines; angle maps to k via k_par = (E/hbar c) sin θ. (4) Hopfield coefficients: plot |C_phot|^2 and |C_exc|^2 (each 0 to 1, summing to 1) vs detuning (or vs k); the LP photon fraction goes 1->0 as detuning goes negative->positive, the two curves cross at 0.5 at resonance. (5) Bloch sphere: unit sphere, north pole = |0> (ground), south pole = |1> (excited), state |psi>=cos(θ/2)|0>+e^{iφ}sin(θ/2)|1>, axes = <σx>,<σy>,<σz>; surface = pure states, interior = mixed. (6) Marcus parabolas: free energy G (or E) vs reaction coordinate q; two identical-curvature parabolas (reactant, product) offset vertically by driving force ΔG° (=ΔE) and horizontally; reorganization energy λ = vertical drop from reactant min to product curve at reactant geometry; barrier ΔG‡=(ΔG°+λ)^2/(4λ) at the crossing; a cavity adds Rabi-split (hΩ) product surfaces (LP/UP/dark) that lower the effective barrier — canonical figure Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo et al. Nat. Commun. 10 (2019) 4685 Fig. 3. (7) Rate vs N / collective coupling: plot rate ratio k_in/k_out vs collective coupling g√N (or vs N); bell-shaped curves that rise then turn over (the LP-channel turnover), strongest near resonance — canonical figure Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo Nat. Commun. Fig. 4; the resonance version plots k/k0 vs cavity frequency in cm^-1 with a sharp peak at the vibrational frequency. (8) Jablonski/energy-level diagram of collective coupling: a ground state |g,0> at bottom, then a manifold at the bare excitation energy split by the cavity into a lower polariton (LP) and upper polariton (UP) separated by Ω_R = 2g√N, with the (N-1) DARK states drawn as a dense band of levels (the 'dark-state reservoir') sitting between LP and UP at the bare molecular energy.

key_equations.name: Upper/lower polariton energies (coupled two-oscillator / Jaynes-Cummings)

variables.meaning: upper (+) and lower (-) polariton branch energy
bare exciton energy (flat in k for QW exciton)
bare cavity photon energy, increases with angle/momentum
variables.typical: detuned within tens of meV of E_X

variables.meaning: vacuum Rabi splitting = minimum branch separation at resonance
variables.typical: 7-9 meV (GaAs QW); up to several 100 meV (organics)

key_equations.where: Houdre, Stanley, Oesterle, Weisbuch, C. R. Physique 3 (2002) 15-27 (coupled-oscillator anticrossing); explicit angular form e.g. arXiv:2110.13456
key_equations.context: Strong-coupling regime, two coupled oscillators (one cavity photon, one exciton); valid near resonance, rotating-wave approximation. hΩ is the diagonalized off-diagonal coupling (often written V or g; full splitting at resonance).

key_equations.name: Cavity photon angular dispersion (angle-to-energy mapping)

variables.meaning: cavity mode energy at normal incidence (k_par=0)

key_equations.where: e.g. Lee et al. / organic microcavity literature, arXiv:2110.13456; standard coupled-oscillator fit
key_equations.context: Planar Fabry-Perot cavity; the photon disperses parabolically near k=0 (E_C ≈ E_C0(1 + sin^2θ/(2 n_c^2))). This is why angle (deg) and in-plane k are interchangeable twin axes.

key_equations.name: In-plane momentum from emission angle

variables.meaning: in-plane wavevector (horizontal axis of dispersion plots)

key_equations.where: Houdre et al., C. R. Physique 3 (2002) 15-27, Fig. 2 (twin angle/wavevector axes); standard angle-resolved spectroscopy relation
key_equations.context: Far-field angle-resolved PL/reflectivity: each external angle maps one-to-one to an in-plane k. Underlies both the dispersion plot (1) and the angle-resolved PL map (3).

key_equations.name: Hopfield coefficients (photon and matter fractions, normalized)
key_equations.where: Bhatt, Kowalewski, et al. review 'Molecular Polaritons...' Chem. Rev. / PMC10941193; Houdre et al. C.R. Physique 3 (2002) (mixing coefficients C_ij = 1/sqrt(2) at resonance)
key_equations.context: From diagonalizing the 2x2 coupled-oscillator Hamiltonian; eigenvector components squared. Plotted vs detuning or vs k. At resonance both = 0.5; far off resonance one branch becomes pure photon, the other pure exciton.

key_equations.name: Bloch sphere state parametrization
key_equations.where: Bloch sphere, Wikipedia (and Nielsen & Chuang convention)
key_equations.context: Two-level system. Cartesian axes = <σ_x>, <σ_y>, <σ_z> (Bloch vector). Density matrix rho = (I + r·σ)/2, |r|<=1; surface (|r|=1) = pure states, interior = mixed states. North |0> / south |1> is the standard but arbitrary convention.

key_equations.name: Marcus diabatic free-energy parabolas

variables.meaning: reactant and product diabatic free energies — vertical axis
driving force = vertical offset of product minimum (ΔE in cavity papers)

key_equations.where: Tokmakoff, MIT, 'Marcus Theory for Electron Transfer', Chemistry LibreTexts ch.19.4
key_equations.context: Two intersecting parabolas of EQUAL curvature; reorganization energy lambda = curvature x (displacement)^2 = vertical gap at reactant geometry. Diagram axes: free energy G vs reaction coordinate q.

key_equations.name: Marcus activation barrier

variables.meaning: activation free energy (barrier height at parabola crossing)

key_equations.where: Tokmakoff / LibreTexts 19.4; Marcus, Rev. Mod. Phys. 65 (1993) 599 (Nobel lecture)
key_equations.context: Normal regime when -ΔG°<λ; barrierless when -ΔG°=λ; inverted regime when -ΔG°>λ (barrier reappears). The vertex of the parabola crossing is the barrier marked on the diagram.

key_equations.name: Marcus(-Levich-Jortner) rate constant
key_equations.where: Tokmakoff / LibreTexts 19.4; Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo, Ribeiro, Yuen-Zhou, Nat. Commun. 10 (2019) 4685, Eq. 1 (MLJ form with Huang-Rhys sum)
key_equations.context: Nonadiabatic (weak-coupling) limit. The MLJ generalization adds a Franck-Condon-weighted sum over high-frequency vibrational quanta v. Cavity reshapes the product surfaces into LP/UP/dark channels each with its own ΔG‡.

key_equations.name: Collective polariton energies and Rabi splitting (Tavis-Cummings)

variables.meaning: collective vacuum Rabi splitting, scales as sqrt(N)

key_equations.where: Bhatt et al. review PMC10941193; Mandal, Huo et al. 'Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry' Chem. Rev. 123 (2023) 9786; Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo et al. Nat. Commun. 10 (2019) 4685, Eq. 4 (omega_±(N))
key_equations.context: N two-level emitters + 1 cavity mode. Two bright polaritons (LP, UP) plus (N-1) DARK states at the bare molecular energy omega_0. The sqrt(N) enhancement is why ensembles reach strong coupling. Drawn on the energy-level/Jablonski diagram.

facts.claim: The canonical polariton dispersion plot (Houdre et al. Fig. 2, from Phys. Rev. Lett. 73 (1994) 2043) puts Energy (eV) on the vertical axis (range ~1.31-1.36 eV), in-plane Wave vector (m^-1) on the bottom axis (0 to ~4x10^6 m^-1), and a TWIN top axis 'External emission angle (deg)' from -10 to +40; the LP and UP data (red points) avoid-cross the dashed bare-exciton (flat) and bare-cavity (parabolic) lines, with the Rabi splitting Omega = 7.1 meV marked by a vertical double-headed arrow at the closest approach.
facts.source: Houdre, Stanley, Oesterle, Weisbuch, 'Strong coupling regime in semiconductor microcavities', C. R. Physique 3 (2002) 15-27, Fig. 2

facts.claim: The vacuum Rabi splitting appears in absorption/reflectivity as a doublet of two peaks separated by Omega; for high-quality InGaAs GaAs QW microcavities Omega is ~7-9 meV (e.g. Omega=8.8 meV in absorption at T=100 K), linewidths gamma a few meV, plotted as Absorption vs Energy (eV) ~1.33-1.35 eV.
facts.source: Houdre et al., C. R. Physique 3 (2002), Fig. 1 (from Phys. Rev. B 49 (1994) 16761)

facts.claim: Rabi splitting scales as Omega ∝ sqrt(f_osc N_QW / L_cavity) for inorganic QWs and as Omega_R = 2g sqrt(N) for molecular ensembles; this sqrt(N) collective enhancement is the standard reason ensembles (N~10^6-10^12 per mode) reach strong coupling and is the key axis-scaling behind rate-vs-collective-coupling plots.
facts.source: Houdre et al. C.R. Physique 3 (2002); Bhatt et al. 'Molecular Polaritons...' (PMC10941193); Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo et al. Nat. Commun. 10 (2019) 4685

facts.claim: Anticrossing vs detuning is conventionally shown by plotting the two measured polariton peak energies as a function of cavity-exciton detuning (swept from negative through zero to positive); the branches form two hyperbola-like curves whose minimum separation at zero detuning equals the Rabi splitting, with the bare cavity and bare exciton drawn as crossing straight lines. The Houdre review explicitly states the eigenstate energies 'vs. detuning delta (delta = E_phot - E_at) show[] an anticrossing behavior near resonance.'
facts.source: Houdre et al., C. R. Physique 3 (2002) 15-27, Section 1; ResearchGate 'Bragg polariton branches vs detuning showing characteristic anticrossing'

facts.claim: Angle-resolved photoluminescence is plotted as a 2D intensity map I(E, theta): emission energy (eV) on one axis and emission angle theta (deg) on the other; PL intensity is shown with a hot/sequential color map; the fitted LP, UP, bare-cavity, and bare-exciton dispersion curves are overlaid as lines. The angle axis maps one-to-one to in-plane momentum because each emission angle corresponds to one k_parallel.
facts.source: Web search of microcavity ARPL literature (ResearchGate angle-resolved PL spectra figures); Houdre et al. C.R. Physique 3 (2002) (twin angle/k axes)

facts.claim: Hopfield coefficients are plotted as |C_phot|^2 and |C_exc|^2 (each between 0 and 1, summing to unity) versus detuning or versus k; the LP photon fraction decreases from 1 to 0 as detuning goes from negative to positive and the two fractions cross at 0.5 at resonance (where the mixing coefficients C_ij = 1/sqrt(2)).
facts.source: Houdre et al. C. R. Physique 3 (2002) (C_ij = 1/sqrt(2) at resonance, |C_ij|^2 weighting); Bhatt et al. review (PMC10941193)

facts.claim: The standard Bloch sphere convention places the ground state |0> at the north pole and the excited state |1> at the south pole, with |psi>=cos(theta/2)|0>+e^{i phi}sin(theta/2)|1>, theta in [0,pi] the polar angle and phi in [0,2pi) the azimuthal angle; Cartesian axes are <sigma_x>, <sigma_y>, <sigma_z>; surface points are pure states and interior points (rho=(I+r.sigma)/2, |r|<=1) are mixed states.
facts.source: 'Bloch sphere', Wikipedia (en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bloch_sphere); Nielsen & Chuang convention

facts.claim: Marcus diagrams plot free energy G (vertical, energy units) vs reaction coordinate q (horizontal); two intersecting parabolas of equal curvature represent reactant and product diabatic states, offset vertically by the driving force Delta G-degrees and horizontally by their displaced minima; the reorganization energy lambda is the vertical gap at the reactant geometry and the activation barrier Delta G-double-dagger = (Delta G-deg + lambda)^2 / (4 lambda) sits at the crossing. Inverted regime occurs when -Delta G-deg > lambda.
facts.source: Tokmakoff (MIT), 'Marcus Theory for Electron Transfer', Chemistry LibreTexts 19.4

facts.claim: To show how a cavity reshapes the barrier, the canonical figure (Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo et al. Fig. 3) plots potential energy E vs the slow coordinate q_S with the reactant parabola, the product ground-state parabola (in the Marcus inverted regime, drawn orange), and Rabi-split product manifolds: lower polariton (red), upper polariton (purple) separated by hΩ, plus the dense set of dark states (green); labeled energies are the barrier E‡_000, the driving force ΔE, the Rabi splitting hΩ, the vibrational quantum hω_P, the reorganization energy λ_S, and the displacement d_S.
facts.source: Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo, Ribeiro, Yuen-Zhou, 'Resonant catalysis of thermally-activated chemical reactions with vibrational polaritons', Nat. Commun. 10 (2019) 4685, Fig. 3

facts.claim: Rate vs collective coupling / N is conventionally plotted as the ratio of in-cavity to out-of-cavity rate coefficient k_in/k_out versus the collective coupling g*sqrt(N)/omega_P at several detunings; the curves are bell-shaped (turnover): the rate rises as the lower-polariton channel activation energy falls, then turns over and drops once the LP channel enters the Marcus inverted regime, with the maximum largest near resonance. (Calculations in the source span 1 <= N <= 10^11.)
facts.source: Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo et al., Nat. Commun. 10 (2019) 4685, Fig. 4

facts.claim: For vibrational-strong-coupling rate-vs-detuning, the convention is to plot the rate modification k/k0 (dimensionless ratio) versus cavity frequency omega_c (in cm^-1) showing a SHARP resonance peak centered exactly at the molecular vibrational frequency (e.g. resonance at omega_c = omega_0 = 1190 cm^-1), reproducing the experimental observation that effects are maximal at light-matter resonance.
facts.source: Ying, Mandal, Huo et al., 'Resonance theory of vibrational polariton chemistry at the normal incidence', PNAS / PMC11636501, Fig. 5(c)

facts.claim: In the collective-coupling energy-level (Jablonski-style) diagram, N molecules + 1 cavity mode produce two bright polariton states (lower polariton LP and upper polariton UP) split by Omega_R = 2g*sqrt(N), plus a manifold of (N-1) DARK states that lie at the bare molecular transition energy between LP and UP; for large N this 'dark-state reservoir' vastly outnumbers the two bright states and is drawn as a dense band of levels.
facts.source: Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo et al. Nat. Commun. 10 (2019) 4685 (two polaritons + N-1 dark states, Eqs. 3-4); Mandal, Huo et al. Chem. Rev. 123 (2023) 9786; Bhatt et al. review PMC10941193

facts.claim: In MLJ vibropolariton theory the collective polariton frequencies are omega_±(N) = (omega_0+omega_P)/2 ± Omega_N/2 with Omega_N = sqrt(4 g^2 N + Delta^2), the dark states omega_D = omega_P, and the single-molecule coupling g = -mu sqrt(hbar omega/(2 V epsilon_0)); these define the energies placed on the level diagram and the activation energies of each reactive channel.
facts.source: Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo, Ribeiro, Yuen-Zhou, Nat. Commun. 10 (2019) 4685, Eqs. 3-4 and surrounding text

facts.claim: Color/line conventions in the dispersion literature: lower polariton drawn in red/lower color and upper polariton in blue/purple; bare exciton and bare cavity drawn as dashed/dotted guide lines (flat exciton, parabolic cavity); 2D PL/reflectivity maps use a perceptually-uniform or hot color map for intensity rather than rainbow. (General convention inferred across the cited figures.)
facts.source: Houdre et al. C. R. Physique 3 (2002), Figs. 2,5; Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo Nat. Commun. Fig. 3

sources.title: Strong coupling regime in semiconductor microcavities (Houdre, Stanley, Oesterle, Weisbuch)
Resonant catalysis of thermally-activated chemical reactions with vibrational polaritons (Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo, Ribeiro, Yuen-Zhou)
Molecular Polaritons for Chemistry, Photonics and Quantum Technologies (review)
Theoretical Advances in Polariton Chemistry and Molecular Cavity Quantum Electrodynamics (Mandal, Taylor, Weight, Koessler, Li, Huo)
Resonance theory of vibrational polariton chemistry at the normal incidence
Toward collective chemistry by strong light-matter coupling
Marcus Theory for Electron Transfer (Tokmakoff)
Bloch sphere
Helical polariton lasing from topological valleys in an organic crystalline microcavity (cavity angular dispersion formula)
sources.type: primary paper (arXiv) for E_C(theta) angle-energy mapping

uncertainties: Exact color maps are rarely standardized in text: I inferred the LP=red/lower, UP=blue/purple convention and the use of perceptually-uniform/hot maps for 2D PL maps from the cited figures rather than an explicit style guide. A simulation should avoid rainbow/jet and use viridis/inferno-like maps, but no single authoritative source mandates a specific colormap.

For the angle-resolved PL map (item 3) I relied on web-search summaries of multiple microcavity ARPL figures rather than fetching one canonical open-access figure with its exact axis labels; the energy-vs-angle intensity-map convention is well established but the specific representative paper/figure (e.g. Kasprzak et al. Nature 2006 condensate maps, or Sanvitto/Deng/Yamamoto reviews) was not directly opened here.

The Mandal/Huo Chem. Rev. 2023 (item 8 energy-level diagram) and the Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo Nat. Commun. main HTML were paywalled/auth-gated to WebFetch; the energy-level/dark-state diagram details were taken from the open arXiv version of Campos-Gonzalez-Angulo (1902.10264), the open PMC review, and the arXiv 'Toward collective chemistry' paper, which are consistent, but I did not visually confirm the exact Chem. Rev. figure number for the LP/UP/dark Jablonski diagram.

The single-molecule coupling sign/normalization g = -mu sqrt(hbar omega/(2 V epsilon_0)) and the precise definition of Omega vs g (factor-of-2 conventions: full splitting 2g vs g) vary between communities (atomic/JC vs semiconductor vs chemistry); a simulation should state explicitly whether Omega denotes the full splitting (used here) or the half-splitting.

Typical numerical ranges (e.g. eV window, k up to ~4x10^6 m^-1, Rabi 7-9 meV) are taken from the specific GaAs/InGaAs systems in Houdre et al.; organic, perovskite, and TMD systems have larger Rabi splittings (100s of meV) and different energy windows (2-3 eV), so axis ranges should be set per material system.

uncertainties.from: A polariton graph simulator
uncertainties.note: CRITICAL SCOPE MISMATCH: This paper does NOT contain a Hopfield/coupled-oscillator photon-exciton model. There is NO 2x2 photon-exciton coupling matrix, NO lower/upper polariton dispersion E_LP(k) or E_UP(k), NO vacuum Rabi splitting equation, NO cavity photon dispersion E_cav(k)=sqrt(...), and NO explicit Hopfield coefficients (excitonic/photonic fractions) anywhere in the text. The paper is a driven-dissipative Gross-Pitaevskii / complex Ginzburg-Landau (cGLE) treatment of polariton CONDENSATES mapped onto the classical XY and Kuramoto models. The requested Hopfield-model content is simply not present in this article; those concepts are only alluded to in the introduction ('strong exciton-photon coupling regime', ref [15] Weisbuch et al). To emulate Rabi-split polariton dispersions one would need a different/companion reference.

The physical splitting equals 2g either way, but symbol conventions differ between papers.

uncertainties.note: Dimensional consistency of Eq. (44): the paper writes N_max = 1 + hbar*omega_c/|T_01|^2, but for this to be dimensionless |T_01|^2 must carry units of energy (not energy^2). Reconciling with Eq. (26), where (N-1)|T_01|^2 has units of energy^2 and the barrier-less condition Omega_+ = sqrt((N-1)|T_01|^2) = hbar*omega_c gives N-1 = (hbar*omega_c)^2/|T_01|^2, suggests either a units/normalization convention in the paper or a notational compression in Eq. (44). I could not fully resolve whether |T_01|^2 in Eq. (44) implicitly absorbs a factor of hbar*omega_c. The qualitative scaling (N_max grows with the gap/photon energy at resonance and inversely with the squared photon-coupling) is unambiguous; the exact prefactor convention should be checked against the paper's own units before hard-coding in a simulation.

A simulation should treat resonance (Delta = hbar*omega_c - E_AD = 0) carefully and not introduce a spurious additional E_AD multiplier.

uncertainties.from: web:The scientific controversy and open questions in polaritonic / vibrational-strong-coupling (VSC) chemistry: Ebbesen-group claims that VSC modifies ground-state thermal chemistry, the theoretical "large N" / dark-state difficulty, failed and partial reproductions, and what is currently settled vs unsettled.
uncertainties.note: Whether the field has reached genuine 'consensus' on any reproducible positive VSC effect is itself contested. Some groups (Ebbesen, and later Nature Chemistry 2024 work) maintain robust resonant effects exist; critics maintain most are artifacts or irreproducible. I have presented this as a live disagreement rather than declaring a winner.

The statement that 'no theory yet reproduces all three' is my synthesis and could be outdated by very recent (2025-2026) work I did not retrieve.
